En: So you also killed.
Zh: “所以你也杀人了。”

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。

En: As retaliation, their rifleman tried shooting you. He hit the cuirass... I heard it go off. I was looking for a clear line of sight to him.
Zh: “作为报复，他们的步枪手想朝你开枪。他打中了你的胸甲……我听到响声了。当时我正在寻找能看见他，视线清晰的地方。”

En: As retaliation, their rifleman shot you. He hit. With his carabine -- I was looking for a clear line of sight. When I found it...
Zh: “作为报复，他们的步枪手朝你开了一枪。他打中了——用他的卡宾枪。当时我正在寻找能看见他，视线清晰的地方。当我发现的时候……”

En: He sounds a *tiny bit* sorry he did not find it before you got hit.
Zh: 他听起来*有些*抱歉，自己没能抢在你中枪之前发现。

En: What happened? You shot the colonel -- unsuccessfully. Then you threw your gun at him. A firefight ensued.
Zh: “发生什么事了？你朝上校开枪——不过失败了。然后你把枪朝他扔了过去。接着双方开始交火。”

En: What happened? You shot the major. Unsuccessfully. A firefight ensued.
Zh: “发生什么事了？你朝少校开枪。失败了。然后双方开始交火。”

En: What happened? You threw an improvised petroleum bomb at the major. A firefight ensued.
Zh: “发生什么事了？你朝少校扔了一个临时做的烈酒炸弹。然后双方开始交火。”

En: You tried to throw an improvised petroleum bomb. It missed. Then you said *it's a fiasco, bratan* in a funny voice. A firefight ensued.
Zh: “你朝少校扔了一个临时做的烈酒炸弹。没打中。接着你用一种很滑稽的声音说了句：*真是大失败啊，兄弟*。然后双方开始交火。”

En: The tie made me do it.
Zh: “是领带让我这么做的。”

En: He does not answer, and searches for something in his coat pocket instead.
Zh: 他没有回应，而是在上衣口袋里翻找着什么。

En: What happened? We tried to take the diplomatic route and hoped they wouldn't attack first. They did. The major gave the command.
Zh: “发生什么事了？我们想试着走外交路线，希望他们不会先动手。不过他们还是这么做了。是少校下的命令。”

En: Titus is dead?
Zh: “提图斯死了？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: You were bleeding out. I think you said something about your wife... And you *warned* me. I was able to disarm the major before he got the jump on me.
Zh: “你血流不止。我想你说了些关于你妻子的话……而且你*警告*了我。我抢在科顿艾尔之前解除了他的武装。”

En: You were bleeding out. I think you said no one wants to party with you. And you *warned* me. I was able to disarm the major before he got the jump on me.
Zh: “你血流不止。我想你说什么没人想跟你一起派对。而且你*警告*了我。我抢在少校之前解除了他的武装。”

En: You were bleeding out. I think you said you lied about being an amnesiac. And you *warned* me. I was able to disarm the major before he got the jump on me.
Zh: “你血流不止。我想你说什么自己失忆是假装的。而且你*警告*了我。我抢在少校之前解除了他的武装。”

En: You were bleeding out by then... He thinks. "You warned me -- I was able to disarm Kortenaer before he got the jump on me."
Zh: “那时候你血流不止……”他想了想。“你警告了我——我抢在科顿艾尔之前解除了他的武装。”

En: And the major...
Zh: “那个少校……”

En: ... is in a private hospital across the river. Krenel claimed him from the local butcher shop, where Titus died. Turns out he's *insured*.
Zh: “……在河对岸的一家私人医院里。科奈尔在当地的肉铺里找到了他，提图斯死在了那里。结果他做了*保险措施*。”

En: We won't get to him anymore. The good news is -- he's not coming here either. I did some damage.
Zh: “我们再也找不到他了。好消息是——他也不会再回到这里。我还是造成了一点伤害的。”

En: There is unveiled anger in his voice.
Zh: 他的声音里充满着赤裸裸的愤怒。

En: Four: Glen, Theo, Angus. The fat one, he... took a lot of bullets. There's a pause. "And Titus."
Zh: “四个：格伦，特奥，安格斯，那个胖子，他……中了很多枪。”他停顿了一下。“还有提图斯。”

En: Five: Glen, Theo, Angus. The fat one, he... took a lot of bullets. There's a pause. "And Titus."
Zh: “五个：格伦，特奥，安格斯，那个胖子，他……中了很多枪。”他停顿了一下。“还有提图斯。”

En: Three: Glen, Theo, Angus. The fat one, he... took a lot of bullets. There's a pause. "And Theo -- he was just too old for combat."
Zh: “三个：格伦，特奥，安格斯。那个胖子，他……中了很多枪。”他停顿了一下。“还有特奥——他太老了，不适合上战场。”

En: And... he smiles: "We are still alive -- both of us."
Zh: “而且……”他笑了：“我们还活着——两个人都活下来了。”

En: Let's face it, officer, he says calmly -- without accusation. "It could have gone a little better. You could've *not* missed."
Zh: “面对现实吧，警官，”他的声音很平静——没有谴责的意味。“本来情况可以稍微好一点。你本来*不该*失手的。”

En: Let's face it, officer... he says calmly -- without accusation. "It could have gone a little better. You could've *not* missed with the bomb. If you already threw it."
Zh: “面对现实吧，警官，”他的声音很平静——没有谴责的意味。“本来情况可以稍微好一点。有了那个炸弹，你本来*不该*失手的。毕竟你都扔出去了。”

En: Let's face it, officer... he says calmly -- without accusation. "It could have gone a little better. You could have been *armed*. It was my mistake too, I should have attacked..."
Zh: “面对现实吧，警官，”他的声音很平静——没有谴责的意味。“本来情况可以稍微好一点。你本来可以带上*武器*的。这也是我的错，我应该动手的……”

En: Let's face it, officer... he says calmly -- without accusation. "It could have gone a little better. The diplomatic approach was terrible. It was my mistake. I should have attacked..."
Zh: “面对现实吧，警官……”他的声音很平静——没有谴责的意味。“本来情况可以更好一些。外交手段太可怕了。这是我的错。我应该动手的……”

En: He was afraid.
Zh: 他很害怕。

En: What happens now, Kim?
Zh: “现在会怎么样，金？”

En: Reasonably bad. You were shot in the left quadriceps -- that's your thigh. It appears no major arteries were nicked. The bullet was removed and a bacterial infection treated with mercurochrome.
Zh: “相当糟糕。你左侧的四头肌中枪了——就是你的大腿。看来主动脉都没有被割伤。子弹已经取出来了，还用红药水治疗了细菌感染。”

En: Can I... walk?
Zh: “我还……能走吗？”

En: We will see.
Zh: “等以后看吧。”

En: With considerable pain and the stitches tearing every now and then -- you should be able to do it.
Zh: 伴随着相当大的疼痛，还有时不时撕裂的缝线——你应该能做到。

En: Through tears and blood and stitches tearing it *should* be possible.
Zh: 通过血和泪，还有那撕裂的缝线，*应该*是可能做到的。

En: If it's possible, then by pure willpower alone. You are going to have to become... a psycholocomotor.
Zh: 如果可能的话，那就只能是依靠纯粹的意志力。你会变成……一个心灵电动马达。

En: The bruising in your shoulder is negligible. The armour took the brunt of the fire.
Zh: 他指着你的肩膀：“你肩膀上的擦伤可以忽略不计。装甲承受了火力的冲击。”

En: Pretty bad, officer. You've suffered two wounds. The first is below your shoulder. The bullet passed through your shoulder blade, luckily missing your lung -- and heart.
Zh: “挺糟糕的，警官。你有两处伤口。第一个在肩膀下面。子弹穿过你的肩胛骨，幸运的是没有打中你的肺部——还有心脏。”

En: The second shot hit you in the thigh -- the left quadriceps. He points to your leg. "No major arteries were nicked, but the bullet had to be removed. A bacterial infection was treated with mercurochrome."
Zh: “另一处在你的大腿——左边的四头肌。”他指着你的腿说。“主动脉没有被擦伤，子弹已经取出来了。还用红药水治疗了细菌感染。”

En: Reasonably bad. You were shot in the left quadriceps -- that's your thigh. The outer side thankfully, no major arteries were nicked. The bullet was removed and a bacterial infection treated with mercurochrome.
Zh: “相当糟糕。你左侧的四头肌中枪了——就是你的大腿。幸运的是，外侧主动脉没有被擦伤。子弹已经取出来了。还用红药水治疗了细菌感染。”

En: You won't be able to dance much, that's for sure. You should be able to live with limping around though.
Zh: 可以肯定的是，你应该没什么办法跳舞了。不过一瘸一拐走路还是可以的。

En: I'm a psycholocomotor.
Zh: “我是个心灵电动马达。”

En: Good. You'll need to be... whatever that is.
Zh: “很好。你是的……不管那到底是什么东西。”

En: Has anyone from my station been to see me?
Zh: “我的警局有人来看过我吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: If not my station, then who treated me?
Zh: “如果不是我的警局，那是谁帮我治的？”

En: I did.
Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: I didn't know you could... do that.
Zh: “我不知道你还……会这个。”

En: No need.
Zh: “不用。”

En: It's part of a detective's task chain. You can do it too.
Zh: “这是警探任务链的一环。你也可以做到。”

En: They don't care about me at all.
Zh: “他们一点都不关心我。”

En: Good. I don't need them.
Zh: “很好。我也不需要他们。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: Are you hurt?
Zh: “你受伤了吗？”

En: Isn't that... strange?
Zh: “难道不觉得……有些奇怪吗？”

En: I called your station after the fight. The injury was logged in. They told me they've sent officers to join you on the site.
Zh: “交火结束之后，我给你的警局去了电话。受伤情况已经登记过了。他们告诉我会派警官去现场跟你会合。”

En: Odd. You haven't seen any, have you?
Zh: “奇怪。你没看见他们，不是吗？”

En: There's a pause. "I'm sure they're worried about you."
Zh: 他停顿了一下。“我确定他们是担心你的。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: I'm sure they're worried about you.
Zh: “我确定他们是担心你的。”

En: No. They would be here if they were truly worried.
Zh: 不对。如果他们真的担心，现在就应该在这里。

En: That means he hasn't seen them around while you were out. They're not *really* worried about you. If they were, wouldn't they be here?
Zh: 这就意味着在你昏迷的时候他没有在附近看到他们。他们并不是*真正*担心你。要是真担心，他们不应该在吗？

En: Not very. He rubs his chin. "I have a concussion from that woman beating me with the butt of her gun... I try to not move too much."
Zh: “不算吧。”他揉揉下巴。“我被那女人的枪托砸了一下，有点脑震荡……我尽量不多动。”

En: Not very. He rubs his chin. "I have a concussion from the major beating me with the butt of his gun. I try to not move too much."
Zh: “不算吧。”他揉揉下巴。“我被少校的枪托砸了一下，有点脑震荡。我尽量不多动。”

En: Things would be worse if you didn't warn me. There's a pause. "Thank you. I did not see her coming. Stupid of me..."
Zh: “如果你没有警告我，情况说不定会更糟糕。”他停顿了一下。“谢谢你。我没想到她会来这一下子。我真是太蠢了……”

En: Things would be much worse if you didn't warn me. There's a pause. "Thank you. I did not see him coming. Stupid of me..."
Zh: “如果你没有警告我，情况说不定会更糟糕。”他停顿了一下。“谢谢你。我没想到他会来这一下子。我真是太蠢了……”

En: If they're so worried about me where are they?
Zh: “如果他们担心我的话，那他们人呢？”

En: Better not agitate yourself further, it already hurts.
Zh: 最好别再让自己激动了，这样真的很疼。

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Sorry.
Zh: 抱歉。

En: Okay.... (Get up.)
Easy now... The lieutenant turns double again, before your eyes. An orange hue of pain...
Zh: “轻一点……”你眼前再次出现了两个警督。一阵橙黄色的痛楚……

En: Your balance is *way* off. You feel like you're about to fall over on that thing...
Zh: 你的平衡能力差了*很多*。你感觉自己要倒下去了……

En: You can take it. Just don't lean on that leg of yours too heavily.
Zh: 你可以承受住的。只要不把太多重量放在大腿上。

En: How are you?
Zh: “你还好吗？”

En: My... disco days are done.
Zh: “我的……迪斯科岁月已经结束了。”

En: I feel fantastic. Let's... rock.
Zh: “我感觉非常好。我们……燥起来吧！”

En: Who cares. Who cares about me. It doesn't matter.
Zh: “谁在乎。谁关心过我。不重要了。”

En: I'm very bad, Kim. Things are very bad.
Zh: “我非常不好，金。情况真的很糟糕。”

En: Gnhhhh...
The lieutenant doesn't reply -- just looks at you teetering on your feet.
Zh: 警督没有回应——只是看着你摇摇晃晃地站着。

En: He thinks you should sit back down. Back straight, now... you're not sitting ANYWHERE.
Zh: 他在想你应该坐下。坐直，就是现在……你没有坐在任何地方。

En: The lieutenant looks at you -- teetering on your feet.
Zh: 警督看着你——摇摇晃晃地站着。

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点点头。

En: Your *disco days* should've been *done* quite a while back, lieutenant-yefreitor.
Zh: “你的*迪斯科岁月*早就应该*结束*了，荣誉警督。”

En: Your disco days should have been done quite a while back, officer.
Zh: “你的迪斯科岁月早就应该结束了，警官。”

En: I honestly don't know.
Zh: “我是真的不知道。”

En: You see the lieutenant's familiar shape in the orange jacket. It turns double -- then triple -- from the pain.
Zh: 你看见警督熟悉的身影，还是穿着那件橙色的夹克。因为痛楚，你看到他变成了两个——然后是三个。

En: Kim....
It's nothing. You're alive, that's what matters.
Zh: 没什么的。你还活着，这才是最重要的。

En: *Sunrise, parabellum*, the lieutenant says. He's in the middle of a freshly cleaned room, with the fan above his head like a halo. His face is covered in bruises.
Zh: “*日出，帕拉贝伦*，”警督说道。他站在一间刚刚打扫干净的房间中央，头顶的风扇像个光环。他的脸上满是淤青。

En: I'm alive...
Zh: “我还活着……”

En: This is because I'm a La Puta Madre's peone, isn't it?
Zh: “这一切都因为我是马德雷教父的代理人，是吗？”

En: You don't... know?
Zh: “你……不知道？”

En: But... I don't know either.
Zh: “但是……我也不知道。”

En: Good, because I totally do.
Zh: “很好，因为我完全知道。”

En: We can't talk to Evrart, the harbour is in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. Joyce has left too, thanks to our meddling.
Zh: “我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。因为我们多管闲事，乔伊斯也离开了。”

En: We can't talk to Evrart, the harbour is in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. Joyce has left too, we never got to know what she would have told us. I'm sure it was important.
Zh: “我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。乔伊斯也走了，我们永远也不会知道她想要告诉我们什么。我肯定挺重要的。”

En: We can't talk to Evrart, the harbour is in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. We never even met the Wild Pines representative, and she's gone too...
Zh: “我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。我们甚至都没见到野松公司的代表，她也离开了……”

En: We can't talk to Evrart, the harbour is in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. And Joyce has left too.
Zh: “我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。而且乔伊斯也离开了。”

En: Joyce is gone?
Zh: “乔伊斯走了？”

En: Yes, she left yesterday morning. To meet the board of Wild Pines -- or that is what I've heard. There's a *pin* somewhere in the machine that keeps Krenel from sending in a death squad... He looks out the window.
Zh: “是的，她昨天早上走的。为了去见野松公司的董事会——至少我是这么听说的。那台机器的某处有一根*插销*在阻止科奈尔派出敢死队……”

En: It's a little late to elicit her support now. Ruby's gone. And Klaasje too. We really should have arrested her, you know.
Zh: 他看向窗外。“现在争取她的支持已经太晚了。现在露比已经走了。卡拉洁也是。你知道吗，我们真的应该逮捕她的。”

En: Maybe it's her? Maybe she kept her end. Either way, Ruby's gone. And Klaasje too. We really should have arrested her, you know.
Zh: 他看向窗外。“也许是她？也许她会信守诺言。不管怎么说，露比走了。卡拉洁也走了。你知道吗，我们真的应该逮捕她的。”

En: Do you? Because we can't talk to Evrart, the harbour's in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. Joyce has left too, thanks to our meddling.
Zh: “是吗？因为我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。因为我们多管闲事，乔伊斯也离开了。”

En: Do you? Because we can't talk to Evrart, the harbour's in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. Joyce has left too; we never got to know what she would have told us.
Zh: “是吗？因为我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。乔伊斯也离开了；我们永远也没办法知道她想告诉我们什么。”

En: Do you? Because we can't talk to Evrart, the harbour's in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. The Wild Pines representative has left too, and we never even met her...
Zh: “是吗？因为我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。野松公司的代表也离开了，我们甚至都没见过她……”

En: Do you? Because we can't talk to Evrart, the harbour is in lockdown -- everyone in there is outside our grasp now. And Joyce has left too.
Zh: “是吗？因为我们没办法跟艾弗拉特说话，港口已经封闭了——里面的每一个人都在我们的掌控之外。而且乔伊斯也离开了。”

En: Titus is *dead*?
Zh: “提图斯*死了*？”

En: Yes. He pulls on his cigarette intently.
Zh: “是的。”他心无旁骛地抽着烟。

En: Who did it then, Kim? Who killed the hanged man?
Zh: “是谁做的，金？是谁杀了吊人？”

En: Honestly I think our investigation has not produced a single credible suspect. His voice is calm, matter-of-factly.
Zh: “说实话，我觉得我们的调查没能发现一个可靠的嫌疑人。”他的声音很冷静，也很实事求是。

En: I don't know. I think your *incredibly dangerous* theory on Titus Hardie was wrong. More than six people saw Titus downstairs that night.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你对提图斯·哈迪那套*极其危险*的理论是错的。那晚有超过6个人在楼下看到了提图斯。”

En: I don't know. I think the *dangerous* theory you presented on Klaasje -- or whatever her name is -- was wrong. We have not found a motive, or a weapon for her.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你提出的卡拉洁——或者随便她叫什么名字——*很危险*的理论是错的。我们没有找到动机，也没有在她那里找到武器。”

En: I don't know. I think the *dangerous* theory you presented on Klaasje was wrong. We have not found a motive, or a weapon for her.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你提出的卡拉洁*很危险*的理论是错的。我们没有找到动机，也没有在她那里找到武器。”

En: I don't know. I think the theory you presented -- it's someone else, outside our circle of suspects -- was right. It'd better be. Everyone within the circle is either dead or gone.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你提出的理论——我们划定的嫌疑人圈子之外的其他人——是正确的。而且这样更好。因为圈子内的所有人不是死了，就是已经走了。”

En: I don't know. I think your *incredibly dangerous* theory about *you* being the killer was incorrect, however. There is not one piece of evidence to support it.
Zh: “我不知道。不过，我觉得你那套*极其危险*的理论——自己才是*凶手*——是不对的。根本没有任何支持这个理论的证据。”

En: I don't know. I think the theory you presented -- on Ruby -- does not add up. If she wasn't on her way to the morgue we could pursue her on many offences -- but not this one.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你提出的理论——凶手是露比——不太说得通。如果她不在去停尸房的路上，我们可以追究她的很多罪行——但杀人不行。”

En: I don't know. I think the theory you presented -- it being Ruby -- did not add up. If we ever find her, we can pursue her on many offences, but not this one.
Zh: “我不知道。我觉得你提出的理论——凶手是露比——不太说得通。如果还能找到她，我们可以追究她的很多罪行，但杀人不行。”

En: That was our job to find out, wasn't it? Honestly, I just don't know. Our circle of suspects is dead and gone. It's not a circle at all. It's a ruin.
Zh: “我们的工作就是查清楚，不是吗？说实话，我真的不知道。我们圈定的嫌疑人死的死，走的走。这已经不是个圈子了，是一片废墟。”

En: Don't be narcissistic. Half the cops in Revachol West are his *peones*. Even if you are, it is not a decisive factor in this case.
Zh: “别自我陶醉了。瑞瓦肖西边有一半警察都是他的*代理人*……就算你是，在这个案子里也不是什么决定性因素。”

En: What about the hole in the... ouch... wall? Someone was checking her out.
Zh: “那个洞呢……好疼……就在墙上那个？有人在偷窥她。”

En: There's still a... 28% possibility the shot came from a distance.
Zh: “还有……28%的可能性，那一枪是从远处射进来的。”

En: An antique bullet... from a Belle-Magrave, 4.46 mm. How hard can it be to find one? How hard can it *be*?
Zh: “一颗古董子弹，出自一把贝莱·马格雷，4.46毫米口径。要找到这样一把枪能有多难？到底*能*有多难？”

En: Something is creeping around out there. I sense it.
Zh: “外面有些鬼鬼祟祟的东西。我能感觉到。”

En: What is?
Zh: “是什么？”

En: The miracle hasn't happened yet. It's not over yet.
Zh: “奇迹还没有发生。一切还没有结束。”

En: It's simple, we have to locate *communism*. Communism killed him.
Zh: “很简单，我们必须找出*康米主义*。是康米主义杀了他。”

En: There are all these... old bunkers and weapon caches. Revolutionary era!
Zh: “还有这些……旧碉堡和武器库。大革命时代的产物！”

En: There was that... hidden bunker under the boardwalk. Revolutionary era!
Zh: “木板道下面……有个隐藏的碉堡。是大革命时期留下来的！”

En: There was that... hidden weapons cache under the building. Revolutionary era!
Zh: “还有那个……建筑下面隐藏的武器库。大革命时代的产物！”

En: There are all these... old bunkers and weapon caches around...
Zh: “附近有这么多的……旧碉堡还有武器库……”

En: We could find thousands more if we wanted. All of Revachol is full of them.
Zh: “如果我们愿意的话，可以找到几千个。整个瑞瓦肖城到处都是。”

En: It's *extremely* easy. There are thousands lying around, *we* found one. All completely unusable. It's precisely how easy it is to find one that makes the bullet useless.
Zh: “*极其*简单。这附近有上千把，到处都是，*我们*也找到了一把。所有全都是坏的。找到一把让子弹派不上用场的步枪就这样简单。”

En: It's extremely easy. There are thousands lying around from the war. All completely unusable. It's precisely how *easy* it is that makes that bullet useless.
Zh: “极其简单。这附近有上千把，到处都是，所有全都是坏的。找到一把让子弹派不上用场的步枪就这样简单。”

En: We should go upstairs, rethink the ballistics *in situ*. A light nod, through some pain. "I agree with this. What else?"
Zh: “我们应该上楼，在*现场*重新考虑一下弹道。”他忍着疼痛，轻轻点点头。“这一点我同意。还有其他的吗？”

En: I don't know... He rubs his neck. "That's been there for years."
Zh: “我不知道……”他摩挲着自己的脖子。“它可能已经在那里好多年了。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: The fucking *May bells*, Kim, the flowers.
Zh: “那个该死的*铃兰*，金，那些花。”

En: The god damn footprints!
Zh: “那该死的脚印！”

En: Yes. He says. "God curse the footprints -- not solving the case for us. *Au diable*."
Zh: “是的。”他说道。“那些该死的脚印——没有帮我们破案。*见鬼去吧*。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: You know what I think about... solving crimes? (Conclude.)
Zh: “你知道我对……破案怎么看的吗？”（总结。）

En: Solving crimes is hard.
Zh: “破案很难。”

En: I'm ready. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我准备好了。”（总结。）

En: No. There's a short pause. "Are you ready to limp?"
Zh: “不。”他短暂地停顿了一下。“你准备好当个瘸子了吗？”

En: Solving crimes is super easy.
Zh: “破案非常简单。”

En: Actually, I want to talk about this crime some more, before I tell you what I think about its hardness.
Zh: “其实，在我告诉你自己对破案难度的看法之前，想再聊聊这起案件。”

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我在听。”

En: He arches his brow -- the ceiling fan patiently spins overhead.
Zh: 他皱起眉头——吊扇不厌其烦地在头顶旋转。

En: I'm ready to hear what you think about solving crimes now.
Zh: “我准备好了，现在你可以说说对破案的看法了。”

En: Solving crimes is almost impossible.
Zh: “破案几乎是不可能的。”

En: It really is *very* hard.
Zh: “它真的*非常*难。”

En: It can feel that way sometimes, yes.
Zh: “我有时候也有这种感觉，没错。”

En: Really? Because, to me, it seems solving crimes is *hard*.
Zh: “真的吗？因为，在我看来，破案好像挺*困难*的。”

En: He sounds surprisingly weary.
Zh: 他的声音意外地疲惫。

En: That concussion must be making him dizzy.
Zh: 那个脑震荡一定是让他昏头了。

En: You're not ready to give up, are you?
Zh: “你还没打算放弃，不是吗？”

En: That's right. Let's give up. Time to start drinking.
Zh: “没错。我们放弃吧。是时候开始喝酒了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Are you ready to limp?
Zh: “你准备好当个瘸子了吗？”

En: I don't think so. There's a short pause. "Are you ready to limp?"
Zh: “我不这么觉得。”他短暂地停顿了一下。“你准备好当个瘸子了吗？”

En: Good. There's a pause. "Where do you want to limp to?"
Zh: “很好。”他停顿了一下。“你这一瘸一拐的想去哪？”

En: It's not *whether* I am -- I am. Jules told me so, he's from my station.
Zh: “问题不是我*是不是*——我就是。朱尔斯就是这么告诉我的，我们一个局里的。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Okay, he repeats.
Zh: “好吧，”他重复到。

En: I've heard people say *I* am on La Puta Madre's take. He looks you in the eye. "And I am *not*."
Zh: “听人说，*我*收了马德雷教父的贿赂。”他直视你的双眼。“但我*没有*。”

En: He is definitely not on La Puta Madre's take.
Zh: 他肯定没有收受马德雷教父的贿赂。

En: They were on the roof. I did not... I did  not catch them. Fucking butterfingers...
Zh: “它们之前就在屋顶上。我没有……我没有抓到它们。手脚真他妈太笨了……”

En: Every piece of garbage in the city is not connected to the case. You don't have to catch *everything*.
Zh: “这个城市的每一个垃圾都与这个案子无关。你没必要抓住*所有东西*的。”

En: (Yank it out and show the dried flower, while it falls to pieces.) "This one, remember?"
Zh: （把它拿出来，亮出支离破碎的干花。）“这一个，还记得吗？”

En: No, Kim. Every piece of garbage in this city *is* connected to the case.
Zh: “不，金。这座城市的每一个垃圾*都与*这个案子有关。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: He's wrong!
Zh: 他错了！

En: Mhm.
Okay, he concedes -- clearly not meaning it.
Zh: “好吧，”他让了一步——但这显然并非他的本意。

En: But they seemed so mysterious.
Zh: 但是它们看起来这么神秘。

En: I can't believe they're fucking useless!
Zh: “我不相信它们他妈的一点用也没有！”

En: I'm just gonna say nothing...
Zh: “我什么也不会说的……”

En: There's a little pause -- he says nothing as well.
Zh: 他短暂地停顿了一下——不发一语。

En: No need to be melodramatic.
Zh: “没必要搞得这么夸张。”

En: *Something*.
Zh: “*某些东西*。”

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: He does not know what to reply. Looks out of the window, then back at you.
Zh: 他不知道该怎么回应。他看了看窗外，又把目光转回到你身上。

En: Hmh... He hums -- thinking about something else.
Zh: “嗯……”他嘟囔着——正在想其他事情。

En: Something, he repeats. It does not sound like he's enthused about this *some-thing*.
Zh: “某些东西，”他重复到。听起来他对*某些东西*不太感兴趣。

En: I'm afraid we may not be able to locate communism, detective -- on account of it being dead.
Zh: “恐怕我们是找不到康米主义的，警探——因为它已经不在了。”

En: Communism is making a *massive* comeback, Kim.
Zh: “康米主义就要*大规模*地回归了，金。”

En: Are you sure? I see communism *everywhere*, telling us what to think, stifling... ouch, my *leg hurts*!
Zh: “你确定吗？我*到处*都能看见康米主义，告诉我们该怎么想，真是让人窒息……哎哟，我的*腿好疼*！”

En: Good. It's good it's dead. Communism is *stupid*.
Zh: “好吧。死了就好。康米主义太*愚蠢*了。”

En: Ideology does not have anything to do with the murder investigation at hand, I'm sorry -- it does not.
Zh: “意识形态跟我们手上正在调查的案子一点关系都没有，很抱歉——但是真的没有。”

En: Okay. Yes. I can see how it's hard to indict someone who is not actually a person.
Zh: “好吧。是的。我可以理解，控诉一个不是真人的东西到底有多难。”

En: I am glad that you do.
Zh: “很高兴你能这么想。”

En: You don't think it was a good idea?
Zh: “你觉得这不是个好主意吗？”

En: I don't know what to think. It might not have been a *bad* idea. There is a *pin* somewhere in the machine. Something is keeping Krenel from sending in a death squad... He looks out the window.
Zh: “我不知道要怎么想。这可能不算是个*坏*主意。那台机器里的某个地方有一根*插销*。某种东西在阻止科奈尔派出一支敢死队……”

En: The... Wild Pines representative?
Zh: “那个……野松公司的代表？”

En: Yes. Joyce Messier. On the boat, you know? She could have helped us. It's clear the mercenaries were working for Wild Pines. At least partially... He looks out the window.
Zh: “是的。乔伊斯·梅西耶。船上那个，你知道吗？她也许能帮到我们。很明显，那些雇佣兵是为野松公司做事的。至少部分是……”

En: It's a little late to elicit her support now. Ruby's gone. And Klaasje -- well, at least she's safely locked away.
Zh: 他看向窗外。“现在争取她的支持已经太晚了。露比走了。还有卡拉洁——呃，至少*她*被安全的藏起来了。”

En: Maybe it's her? Maybe she kept her end... Either way, Ruby's gone. And Klaasje -- well, at least *she's* safely locked away.
Zh: “也许是她？也许她会信守诺言。不管怎么说，露比已经走了。还有卡拉洁——呃，至少*她*被安全地关押起来了。”

En: Locked up? No, she would be of more *use* to us free, honestly. But it was the right thing to do.
Zh: “关在牢房里？不，老实说，如果她没被关起来，对我们会更有*用处*。不过这么做是对的。”

En: A gust of wind blows in from the bay, the duraluminium box around you vibrates imperceptibly... a familiar cold, a red thread on the roof upstairs. Taut. Plucked like a string by the gust.
Zh: 一阵风从海湾吹来，你周围的杜拉铝盒子微微震动着……一种熟悉的寒冷，屋顶上的一根红线。逐渐拉紧。就像一根被狂风拨动的弦。

En: Ballistics. In Klaasje's room. Do it *properly*.
Zh: 弹道学。在卡拉洁的房间里。做得*恰当*一点。

En: Also, it's just close enough to endure the walk.
Zh: 而且距离挺近的，走几步应该能忍得了。

En: Let's just aimlessly wander until a clue presents itself.
Zh: “我们还是就这么漫无目的地闲逛吧，等线索自己出现。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: We should check Mrs. Katarzine Alasije's room, upstairs.
Zh: “我们应该搜查一下卡特琳·艾拉洁小姐的房间，上楼。”

En: We should check Miss Oranje Disco Dancer's room, upstairs.
Zh: “我们应该搜查一下奥兰治那位迪斯科舞者的房间，上楼。”

En: We should check Klaasje's room, upstairs.
Zh: “我们应该搜查一下卡拉洁的房间，上楼。”

En: Let's do that. We can start upstairs in Klaasje's bedroom -- with your ballistics idea. He extinguishes his cigarette on the sole of his boot.
Zh: “就这么办吧。我们可以从楼上卡拉洁的房间开始——试试你的弹道学观点。”他把烟头丢在地上，用鞋底捻灭。

En: Let's do that. We can start upstairs in Klaasje's bedroom -- I have not done a thorough sweep after she left. He extinguishes his cigarette on the sole of his boot.
Zh: “就这么办吧。我们可以从楼上卡拉洁的房间开始——她离开之后，我还没有彻底搜查过。”他把烟头丢在地上，用鞋底捻灭。

En: Let's do that. He extinguishes the cigarette on the sole of his boot.
Zh: “就这么办吧。”他把烟头丢在地上，用鞋底捻灭。

En: Let's do that. We can start upstairs in Klaasje's bedroom -- the ballistics idea... He sighs.
Zh: “就这么办吧。我们可以从楼上卡拉洁的房间开始——那个弹道学的想法……”他叹了口气。

En: Let's do that. We can start upstairs in Klaasje's bedroom -- I have not done a thorough sweep after she left... He sighs.
Zh: “就这么办吧。我们可以从楼上卡拉洁的房间开始——她离开之后，我还没有彻底搜查过……”他叹了口气。

En: Let's do that. He looks to the ceiling. Then the door. "Actually, I'm too tired to walk. Klaasje's room is nearby."
Zh: “就这么办吧。”他看看天花板，然后是大门。“其实，我都累的走不动了。不过卡拉洁的房间就在附近。”

En: Why not... He extinguishes the cigarette on the sole of his boot.
Zh: “为什么不呢……”他把烟头丢在地上，用鞋底捻灭。

En: Why not? He looks to the ceiling, then to the door.
Zh: “为什么不呢。”他看看天花板，然后看向大门。

En: Neither do I. Let's walk and hope something happens before you bleed to death. Klaasje's room is nearby... He extinguishes the cigarette on the sole of his boot.
Zh: “我也不知道。我们走吧，希望在你失血身亡之前，有什么事能够发生。卡拉洁的房间就在附近……”他把烟头丢在地上，用鞋底捻灭。

En: Neither do I. Let's walk and hope something happens, before you bleed to death. Klaasje's room is nearby... He looks to the door.
Zh: “我也不知道。我们走吧，希望在你流血而死之前，有什么事能够发生。卡拉洁的房间就在附近……”他看向大门。

En: I warned them I am a killer of killers...
Zh: “我已经警告过他们了，我是专杀杀人犯的杀手……”

En: That you did. The lieutenant looks at you with a peculiar expression. He then continues...
Zh: “你确实做到了。”警督看着你，表情有些怪异。然后他继续……

En: The lieutenant does not answer, instead searching his coat pockets for something.
Zh: 警督没有回应，而是在上衣口袋里翻找着什么。

En: The tie made me do it.
Zh: “是领带让我这么做的。”

En: If I would have presented the theory better... to their leader...
Zh: “如果我能更好的把这个理论……展示给他们领袖的话……”

En: I did all I could there.
Zh: “我已经竭尽全力了。”

En: I know you did. It's all right.
Zh: “我知道的。没关系。”

En: She never had a chance. She should not have been there in the first place. There was no way to *convince* them.
Zh: “她从没有过机会。她一开始就不应该出现在那里。根本就没办法*说服*他们的。”

En: Stop this line of thought now. You're too broken for ghosts.
Zh: 现在，停止这种想法吧。对于幽灵来说，你实在是太残缺了。

En: The piss-jacket, Kim... you took it off?
The close proximity of death must have made the lieutenant contemplate his life choices. He's *done* with the jacket.
The young woman raises a cigarette to her lips.
Zh: 年轻女子把一支香烟举到嘴边。

En: *Officer*? Am I military personnel?
Zh: “*长官*？我是军事人员吗？”

En: Turn your bloated face away from her beauty and just keep on walking. [Leave.]
Zh: 让你臃肿的脸远离她的美丽，继续向前走。[离开。]

En: Uh... no. She seems perplexed by your question.
Zh: “呃……不是。”她似乎被你的问题难住了。

En: Wait, I know! I'm a businessman. *Chief executive officer*, right?
Zh: “等等，我知道了！我是个商人。*首席执行官*，对吗？”

En: Then why did you call me an officer?
Zh: “那你为什么要叫我长官？”

En: The young woman shakes her head slowly.
Zh: 年轻女子慢慢摇摇头。

En: That's too wild. Chief Technical Officer is what I am.
Zh: “真奇怪啊。我就是首席技术官啊。”

En: Okay... Chief technical officer?
Zh: “好吧……首席技术官？”

En: No... She looks you in the eye. "You're a *police* officer, sir."
Zh: “不……”她凝望你的双眼。“你是一名*警官*，先生。”

En: Because you're a police officer, sir. She pulls on her cigarette.
Zh: “因为你是一名警官，先生。”她抽了一口烟。

En: You're shitting me!
Zh: “你在骗我！”

En: Why would anyone let *me* be an officer of the law?
Zh: “为什么会有人让*我*做执法人员呢？”

En: Who in their right mind would let *me* be an officer of the law?
Zh: “有哪个头脑正常的人会让*我*做执法人员呢？”

En: God damn right, I'm a policeman! And don't you forget it!
Zh: “没错，我是一名警官！你可别给我忘了！”

En: I'm not. Unless *you've* been shitting *us* all this time... She takes another drag.
Zh: “我没有。除非*你*一直都在欺骗*我们*……”她又抽了一口。

En: And what business is that?
Zh: “那是什么事务？”

En: I couldn't say. In truth... so far, mostly drinking.
Zh: “我说不好。说实话……目前为止，你基本就是在喝酒。”

En: There's a mercenary out back. He's been hanged. The body has been there for a week now. The locals probably got tired of it and called the cops.
Zh: 她猛吸了一口，接着开了口。“外面有一个雇佣兵。他被吊死了。尸体已经在那里挂了一个星期。本地人应该是厌倦了吧，所以打电话叫了警察。”

En: I didn't mean to overwhelm you with information. You seem a bit... lost, officer.
Zh: “我并不想用这些信息打击你。你好像有些……迷惘，警官。”

En: Don't be so harsh on yourself. They let almost anyone be a police officer.
Zh: “别对自己太苛刻。差不多什么样的人都能当警官。”

En: Could it be because of the drinking? She raises an eyebrow. The cigarette sizzles.
Zh: “难道是喝酒的原因？”她扬起眉头。香烟在嘶嘶作响。

En: Okay, cool. I won't.
Zh: “好吧，没问题。我不会的。”

En: I should get going now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我该走了。”[离开。]

En: I'm at death's door, bloated, a goner. And still... (Sigh.) "Does the longing ever stop?"
Zh: “我生命垂危，身体臃肿，已经无可救药。不过……”（叹气。）“这种渴望还会停止吗？”

En: She erupts in laughter, all the fatigue swept from her face. "What was that!? That's not even how *words* are used. What did you say? C'mon, say it again!"
Zh: 她突然大笑起来，所有疲劳都从脸上一扫而空。“这是干什么！？你说的话我根本*听不懂*。你刚说什么了？拜托，再说一次！”

En: I said I wanted to have sex with you.
Zh: “我说我想和你上床。”

En: I want to have fuck with you.
Zh: “我想和你来一发。”

En: No, you see, that's not what you said. You said... But she's unable to finish, consumed with laughter.
Zh: “不，知道吗，你刚才不是这么说的。你说……”她还没说完就笑的合不拢嘴。

En: I said *I want to have fuck with you*.
Zh: “我说*我想和你来一发*。”

En: God damn right you did, you crazy asshole, you! She wipes the tears from her eyes. "What kind of cop *are* you?"
Zh: “该死的你确实这么说了，你这个疯子，混账！”她擦干眼里的泪水。“你到底算*哪门子*的警察啊？”

En: I'm a Cop of the Apocalypse.
Zh: “我是末日警探。”

En: Superstar Cop. It's been established.\n
Zh: “明星警探。已经人尽皆知了。”

En: Superstar Cop. I can no longer deny it.
Zh: “明星警探。我还是承认了吧。”

En: I'm sorry. I don't know why I said that. You're pretty... I'm sorry. I'm the Sorry Cop.
Zh: “抱歉。我不知道为什么要这么说。你很漂亮……我很抱歉。我是抱歉警探。”

En: I'm not sure I'm a cop at all. I sure don't remember being one. I think I might have lied.
Zh: “我根本不确定自己到底是不是警察。我确实不记得自己是。我想我可能在撒谎。”

En: Don't be, it was funny. And, anyway, who gives a shit? Who gives a shit about any of it?
Zh: “别这样，其实挺好玩的。再说了，谁他妈在乎？谁在乎这些屁事？”

En: So what if you can't pull Grade A pussy anymore? There are other things in life -- more meaningful, more fitting for a man your age. This... She gestures towards herself, draped in silver. "This is *fata morgana*. One thing, though..." She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “那如果你不能再行房事了怎么办？生命里还有其他东西——更有意义，更适合你这个年纪的人。这个……”身披银装的她，对自己比了个手势。“这只是*海市蜃楼*。不过，有一件事……”她掐灭了手中的香烟。

En: It's gonna suck for you later when you have to interrogate me.
Zh: “如果到了之后你必须审问我的时候，那就糟糕了。”

En: And, for the record: No, I didn't do it.
Zh: “还有，郑重声明一下：不，不是我做的。”

En: No, I don't want to.
Zh: “不，我不想这样。”

En: Has the time come already? She raises an eyebrow.
Zh: “时间已经到了吗？”她扬起一边的眉头。

En: Cops walking around with no memory of who they are, saying what I just said... The end can't be more than two days away.
Zh: “警察到处走来走去，根本不记得他们自己是谁，说着我刚才说过的话……还有最多两天，一切都会结束了。”

En: No, we still have some centuries to go.
Zh: “不，我们还要度过好几个世纪呢。”

En: You're one intense cop. Anyone ever tell you that?
Zh: “你是个很紧张的警察。有人这么告诉过你吗？”

En: I wouldn't know. I have no recollection of anyone ever telling me *anything*.
Zh: “我不会知道的。我不记得有人告诉过我*任何事*。”

En: All right, then. Looks like I should go and prepare for what's to come. And thank you, this has been delightful. I do hope it happens *sooner*. Otherwise... She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “那好吧。看来我应该准备好接下来要发生的事。还有，谢谢你，这次谈话真的挺开心的。我确实希望它发生地*更早一些*。不然的话……”她掐灭手中的香烟。

En: Okay, that's cool. Or... if I can just maybe ask you to elaborate on that superstardom a tiny bit?
Zh: “好吧，那挺酷的。或者……如果可能的话，我能请你再详细说一下你说的那个超级明星的身份吗？”

En: It means I'm a bloated old drunk with sideburns and disco pants. If I don't have a joke up my sleeve -- it's on me.
Zh: “意思是我是个膨胀的老酒鬼，留着连鬓胡子，穿着迪斯科风的裤子。如果我的段子不能张口就来的话——就算我输。”

En: I sincerely think I'm a superstar. It's a theory I'm developing.
Zh: “我真心认为自己是个超级明星。这是我正在研究的一种理论。”

En: I have certainly been entertained. Thank you. Whatever you are, you should stick to it. Otherwise... She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “我真的很开心。谢谢你。不管你是谁，你都应该坚持下去。不然的话……”她掐灭手中的香烟。

En: Okay, sir, I have to ask: do you really not remember? Just like that... She tries to think of a likelier explanation, but fails. "You drank so much you lost your memory?"
Zh: “好吧，先生，我想问：你真的不记得了吗？就像那个……”她想要想出一种可能的解释，但却以失败告终。“你喝了太多酒，然后失去记忆了？”

En: I don't think there's any more to it. I'm a joke of a cop, or I joked about being one, and I drank too much. I'm also old.
Zh: “我觉得差不多吧。我当警察就是个笑话，或者只是我开的一个玩笑，也许是我喝多了。而且，我还太老了。”

En: No. There is a massive, otherworldly mystery afoot here. And I am at the centre of it.
Zh: “不。有一个巨大的、超尘脱俗的秘密正在进行。而我正身处它的中央。”

En: I'm sorry. She puts out her cigarette. "I didn't mean to hurt your feelings."
Zh: “很抱歉。”她熄灭香烟。“我不是有意要伤害你的感情。”

En: You have no doubt about the drinking, but do you strike yourself as a tight-lipped drunk? She must have heard *something*.
Zh: 你在喝酒，这一点毋庸置疑，但你真的给人留下了一种寡言醉汉的形象？她肯定是从别处听到过*什么*。

En: She speaks matter-of-factly, but there's a sadness in her voice.
Zh: 她说话很实事求是，但是声音里略带一丝悲伤。

En: She means it. She wouldn't defy authority, however sweaty and bloated it may look.
Zh: 她是认真的。她不会公然挑战权威——不论权威看起来是如此多汗而臃肿。

En: She hasn't had time to put her make-up on -- this is her morning cigarette. She looks tired, her beauty waning faster than it ought to at her age.
Zh: 她没时间化妆——这是她清晨的第一支烟。她看起来有些疲惫，按照她的年纪，她的美貌消退的快了一些。

En: She appears to genuinely want you to understand it's okay.
Zh: 她好像很真诚地希望你明白——这其实不算什么。

En: Try *The Expression* on her -- let her know you want her. Physically.
Zh: 对她试试那个*表情*——让她知道你想要她。肉体上的。

En: Goddamn right I'm a policeman.
Zh: 没错，我是个警察。

En: Asserted rhetoric.
Zh: 断然的说辞。

En: Stopped *The Expression*.
Zh: 快停下那个*表情*。

En: Alcohol raises testosterone levels, especially in men, she says matter-of-factly. "The levels remain elevated after inebriation ends and the pain begins. You seek comfort. It's only natural."
Zh: “酒精会提高睾丸激素水平，特别是男性的，”她说话实事求是。“在醉酒结束后，这个水平依然很高，接着痛苦就开始了。你在寻求安慰。这很自然。”

En: She puts out her cigarette.
Zh: 她掐灭手中的香烟。

En: Her eyes are brown and her face is speckled with birthmarks. She can't be more than 28.
Zh: 她的眼睛是棕色的，脸上点缀着一些胎记。年纪应该不会超过28岁。

En: 'Officer' is my stage name, right? I can see myself as a middling disco artist called 'The Officer.'
Zh: “‘长官’是我的艺名，对吧？我可以把自己看作是一个名叫‘官员’的二流迪斯科舞者。”

En: *Officer* could be an artistic statement. You're already prone to those.
Zh: *长官*可能是一种艺术陈述。你已经表现出这种倾向了。

En: *Officer* could be an artistic statement. A claim to *official* renown.
Zh: *长官*可能是一种艺术陈述。是*官方*声誉的声明。

En: Are you sure?
Zh: “你确定吗？”

En: I am, yes. Unless you've been feeding us a set of very well rehearsed lies all this time... She takes another drag.
Zh: “是的，我确定。除非你一直都在用精心编造的谎言欺骗我们……”她又抽了一口。

En: Good. Now, how did you know I was a police officer?
Zh: “很好。现在，你怎么知道我是一名警官的？”

En: No need to be alarmed. I was just getting into character, you know.
Zh: “不用这么担惊受怕。你知道的，我只是进入了角色而已。”

En: First I have to ask: Are you okay, sir? All of a sudden, she looks genuinely concerned. "You look like you're about to throw up... Can I bring you something?"
Zh: “首先我要问：你没事吧，长官？”突然间，她表现出真诚的担忧。“你看起来像是要吐出来了……要不要我帮你去拿点什么？”

En: I might not know who I am, but I do know when someone's not telling me the whole story. What am I *doing* here?
Zh: “我也许是不知道自己是谁，但是如果有人有事瞒着我，我还是能分辨出来的。我在这里*做什么*？”

En: I might not know where I am. Or who I am. Or what a *Coupris Kineema* is; how I know what one sounds like...
Zh: “我也许是不知道自己在哪。或者自己是谁。或者*库普瑞斯锐影*是什么；我怎么知道它的声音是什么样的……”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: ...but I do know when someone's not telling me the whole story. What am I doing here? What's my case?
Zh: “……但是如果有人有事瞒着我，我还是能分辨出来的。我在这里做什么？现在手上有什么案子？”

En: Okay. She breathes in the silvery smoke.
Zh: “好吧。”她吸了一口银色的烟雾。

En: How did you know I'm a police officer?
Zh: “你怎么知道我是警官的？”

En: Sir, you've been here for three days. On *official police business*, as you put it.
Zh: “长官，你已经在这里待了3天。你自己说，是来处理*警方事务*的。”

En: She's right. Something wants to come out -- through your mouth. But you can keep it down because your body does not control you.
Zh: 她说的没错。有些东西想出来——从你的嘴里逃出来。但你可以压制住，因为你的身体无法控制你。

En: I'm fine. Answer the question.
Zh: “我没事。你先回答问题吧。”

En: I feel wretched. Tell me, how do you know I'm with the police?
Zh: “我感觉有些难受。告诉我，你怎么知道我是警官的？”

En: It's a small wonder I'm able to even formulate this very thought. But I'll be fine. How did you know I was a cop?
Zh: “我能明确表达出这种想法已经是个小奇迹了。不过我会没事的。你怎么知道我是警官的？”

En: She's right. Something wants to come out through your mouth, right in front of her, right here... It makes you put your hand on your stomach and swallow.
Zh: 她说的没错。有些东西想从你嘴里跑出来，就在她面前，就在这里……你把手放在腹部，把它咽了下去。

En: Sir... For a moment it looks like you're going to blow, but you manage to keep it in. "You've been here for three days. On *official police business*, no less."
Zh: “先生……”有那么一瞬间，你看起来像是要爆炸一样，但你还是控制住了。“你已经在这里待了3天。你自己说，是来处理*警方事务*的。”

En: Sir, you've been here for three days. On *official police business*, as you put it.
Zh: “先生，你已经在这里待了3天。你自己说，是来处理*警方事务*的。”

En: Actually, I can see why they would entrust me with the law. I have the right character.
Zh: “其实，我能理解他们为什么让我做执法人员。我的性格正合适。”

En: No. In the course of our brief conversation I have masterfully suppressed the urge to throw up. This is a sign of professionalism.
Zh: “不。在我们简短的谈话过程中，我熟练地抑制了呕吐的冲动。这是专业精神的标志。”

En: No. In the course of our conversation I have masterfully suppressed the urge to throw up. This is a sign of professionalism.
Zh: “不。在我们的谈话过程中，我熟练地抑制了呕吐的冲动。这是专业精神的标志。”

En: No. I realized you were withholding information. Something lit up in me. A killer instinct.
Zh: “不。我知道你有所隐瞒。某种东西在我身体里点燃。这是一种杀手的嗅觉。”

En: No. I realized you were withholding information. Something lit up in me. A killer instinct.
Zh: “不。我知道你有所隐瞒。某种东西在我身体里点燃。这是一种杀手的嗅觉。”

En: No. I had thrown my shoe out of the window. I *deduced* this from the direction of the shards.
Zh: “不。我把鞋子扔出窗外了。我是从玻璃碎片的方向*推理*出来的。”

En: No. I *deduced* that I'd thrown my shoe out the window from the direction of the shards.
Zh: “不。从玻璃碎片的方向*推断*，我把自己的鞋子扔出窗外了。”

En: No, I get these titbits of information sometimes, little facts like trivia. Gives me a detective-y vibe.
Zh: “不，有时我会得到这些零碎的信息，各种琐碎的事实。给我一种侦探的氛围。”

En: No. My mind keeps coming up with titbits of information -- all sorts of facts, trivia. Gives me a detective-y vibe.
Zh: “不。我的脑子里总是浮现出一些零碎的信息——各种各样的事实，还有琐事。给我一种侦探的氛围。”

En: I think I saw a sadness in you, when you told me about the hanged man. Others would have missed it.\n
Zh: “你跟我提起吊人的时候，我好像在你身上看到了一种悲伤的情绪。要是其他人可能就错过了。”

En: No. I think I saw a sadness in you when you told me about the hanged man. Others would have missed it.
Zh: “不。你跟我提起吊人的时候，我好像在你身上看到了一种悲伤的情绪。要是其他人可能就错过了。”

En: But I wasn't sad. She tilts her head.
Zh: “但是我不悲伤啊。”她歪起头。

En: Yes, what you just said. I can't come up with anything.
Zh: “是的，就是你刚才说的。我什么也想不出来。”

En: Never mind. It's more likely someone made a mistake somewhere...
Zh: “不要紧。更有可能是什么人在其他什么地方犯了错……”

En: Thank you, sir. She smiles, lazily. "I mean that."
Zh: “谢谢你，先生。”她慵懒地笑到。“我是认真的。”

En: Of course. She pulls on her cigarette.
Zh: “当然了。”她吸了一口烟。

En: A fondness for contradictory statements?
Zh: “喜欢互相矛盾的供述？”

En: A glib remark. Don't let it stand -- assert yourself.
Zh: 真是口齿伶俐。别傻站着——表达自己的主张。

En: Better let it go. Say nothing.
Zh: 最好随它去吧。什么也不说。

En: Retreat is not a winning tactic.
Zh: 退避不是胜利的策略。

En: Something stirs in you as she's about to go. Call it an instinct, a need -- the need to ask *questions*. It's like you've said the words a million times before...
Zh: 她打算离开，你心里有些躁动。就当这是一种直觉吧，一种需求——想*问问题*的需求。好像之前你已经说过了千百万次……

En: Something stirs in you as she's about to go. Must be that *killer instinct* again -- the need to ask *questions*. It's like you've said the words a million times before...
Zh: 她打算离开，你心里有些躁动。一定又是那种*杀手的嗅觉*——想*问问题*的需求。好像之前你已经说过了千百万次……

En: Before you go I have some *questions* for you.
Zh: “在你离开之前，我有些*问题*想问你。”

En: What if I don't want to ask questions?
Zh: 如果我不想问问题呢？

En: Of course. She puts out her cigarette. "Be careful, officer. They don't like the police around here."
Zh: “当然。”她吸了一口烟。“小心点，警官。这里的人可不是很喜欢警察。”

En: C'mon man, pretty please! One more time!
Zh: “拜托，老兄！再说一次！”

En: Yes, we are mere minutes away from the total collapse of reality.
Zh: “是的，还剩几分钟，现实就要彻底崩溃了。”

En: Mere minutes? I should go and prepare then. Thank you, this has been delightful. I hope it does all come crashing down. Otherwise... She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “几分钟？那我得走了，抓紧准备。谢谢你，跟你聊天挺开心的。希望它真的会崩溃。不然的话……”她掐灭了手中的香烟。

En: I am a scion of Guillaume le Million.
Zh: “我是纪尧姆·列米利翁的子孙。”

En: Or maybe you *are* Guillaume le Million? He'd be about your age now... Think about it! One thing, though... She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “或者也许你*就是*纪尧姆·列米利翁呢？现在他已经跟你差不多年纪……想想看！不过，有一件事……”

En: Yeah, I guess I'm pretty intense.
Zh: “是啊，我想我真的挺紧张的。”

En: It's obvious. Someone did me over. I got fucked -- bad.
Zh: “很显然。有人狠狠伤害了我。我被搞得——乱七八糟。”

En: I'm sure I'm usually less intense. I don't know why this *stuff* is coming out of my mouth.
Zh: “我确定自己不是经常这么紧张的。我不知道为什么*这种话*会从我嘴里说出来。”

En: You're a cop, Harry. It is *unnatural* of you not to want to ask questions.
Zh: 你是个警官，哈里。如果你不想问问题的话，那才*不正常*呢。

En: Wait! I have some questions before you go.
Zh: “等等！你走之前，我有些问题要问你。”

En: Let the moment pass.
Zh: 让这一刻过去。

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么事？”

En: Where exactly is *here*? Where am I?
Zh: “*这里*到底是什么地方？我在哪？”

En: What is this place? Where am I?
Zh: “这是什么地方？我在哪？”

En: Your room is almost next to mine. Did you hear anything last night?
Zh: “你的房间差不多就在我房间旁边。昨晚你听见什么了吗？”

En: You're in a hostel, sir.
Zh: “你在一间旅社，长官。”

En: No -- *where* are we? Where in the world?
Zh: “不是——我们在*哪里*？这个世界的什么地方？”

En: We are in Revachol.
Zh: “我们在瑞瓦肖。”

En: Revachol is the disgraced former capital of the world, divided into zones of control under foreign occupation -- half a century after a failed world revolution. She is central to our moment in time.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖城是失宠的前世界之都，现在被划分成了几个区域，由外国占领——事情就发生在半个世纪前那场失败的大革命之后。它是我们这个时代的中心。

En: Should that mean something to me?
Zh: “这对我来说有什么意义吗？”

En: Revachol forever.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖万岁。”

En: How about you tell me something else instead?
Zh: “不如你跟我讲点别的吧？”

En: Yeah?
Zh: “你说？”

En: Those words -- "tell me something else" -- feel like something you've said innumerable times before. Your mouth is the very shape of them.
Zh: 那句话——“跟我讲点别的”——感觉你之前已经说过无数遍。甚至你的嘴唇都是那句话的形状。

En: For ever and ever.
Zh: “万万岁。”

En: Tell me, is it '51 now?
Zh: “告诉我，现在是51年吗？”

En: Tell me what year it is.
Zh: “告诉我，今年是哪一年。”

En: What century?
Zh: “什么世纪？”

En: Okay, tell me something else.
Zh: “好吧，跟我讲点别的。”

En: The Current Century.
Zh: “当前的世纪。”

En: And that's what? What number?
Zh: “那又是什么？数字是多少？”

En: Centuries don't have numbers, they have names, and this is the current one.
Zh: “世纪没有数字，它们只有名字，眼下就是当前这个。”

En: How many centuries have there been, then?
Zh: “那已经过去多少世纪了？”

En: Civilization has existed for eight thousand years, sir. She grins.
Zh: “人类文明已经存在8000年了，长官。”她咧开嘴笑了。

En: What's so funny?
Zh: “有什么好笑的吗？”

En: You're right. Her expression turns serious. "There is nothing funny about civilization."
Zh: “你说得对。”她的表情变得严肃起来。“文明没有什么好笑的。”

En: She hates civilization. She feels betrayed by it.
Zh: 她恨人类文明。她感觉被它背叛了。

En: You sure look like you're from Revachol. Revachol parties.
Zh: “你确实看起来像是瑞瓦肖人。瑞瓦肖的派头。”

En: Her accent suggests she is not from around here. She's from Oranje, another part of the world.
Zh: 她的口音不像是本地人。她来自奥兰治，世界的另一边。

En: The dockworkers are pretty cocky. They think they're police enough. At least here on the coast -- I can't say about the rest of the city.
Zh: “码头工人很自大。他们觉得有自己负责治安就够了。至少在海岸这边是这样——城里的其他地方我就不好说了。”

En: What is it that they have against the police here?
Zh: “这里为什么反对警察呢？”

En: There was the usual ruckus. She shrugs. "Loud disco music."
Zh: “还是老一套，闹哄哄的。”她耸耸肩。“很大声的迪斯科音乐。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么了？”

En: Okay. You can go now. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧。你可以走了。”[离开。]

En: Glad to have been of assistance.
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你。”

En: A slow, sad song started playing. Like organ music, on repeat. That went on for quite a while. Some of the time you were yelling along to it.
Zh: “开始播放一首很慢，很悲伤的歌。像是风琴音乐，不断重复，播了好一会儿。有时候，你会跟着音乐一起叫喊。”

En: What was I singing?
Zh: “我在唱什么？”

En: Was I singing this? (Show her the empty case of "The Smallest Church In Saint-Saëns".)
Zh: “我是在唱这个吗？”（把圣桑小教堂的空盒子给她看。）

En: I've heard enough.
Zh: “我已经听够了。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Yes! There was a church in there. A really small church. Like the smallest, saddest church in the whole world. It was about that. And also...
Zh: “是的！歌词里有一座教堂。一座真正的小教堂。好像是整个世界上最小，最悲伤的教堂。唱的就是关于它的事。而且……”

En: That it doesn't *matter* anymore. And that we're *alone* now. It was difficult to tell -- the song itself was very quiet and soft, but you sounded like a wounded boar, sir. It was hard to understand what you were singing on top of it.
Zh: “那个也不*重要*了。现在我们是*孤独的*。这很难讲——那首歌本身很安静，很轻柔，但是你的声音像是一头受伤的野猪，长官。很难理解你唱的到底是什么。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: Then what happened?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: When you say *wounded* do you mean that in a *cool* way? Like a *wild beast*?
Zh: “你说*受伤*，是很*酷*的那种吗？比如说一只*野兽*？”

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: Yes. It was very cool.
Zh: “是的。非常酷。”

En: It's impossible to say whether she's being sarcastic or really means it.
Zh: 很难说她是在讽刺，还是真心实意。

En: Then you started screaming and trashed the place.
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，在屋里闹得天翻地覆。”

En: Don't be, I was going out later anyway. It didn't bother me.
Zh: “不用这样，反正我也会很晚外出。对我来说不算什么困扰。”

En: Are you sure I wasn't being assaulted?
Zh: “你确定我不是被袭击了吗？”

En: That's so *me*. What did I do?
Zh: “还真的挺像*我*会做的事。我做了什么？”

En: You're making this up. I would never behave like that.
Zh: “你瞎编的吧。我绝对不会这么做的。”

En: No, it didn't sound like there was a fight. It sounded like someone was trashing their room.
Zh: “不，听起来不像有打斗的声音。好像是有人在破坏房间。”

En: A window was smashed. The tape player probably -- the song stopped -- and furniture too. A real destructothon. There was screaming. Then, I think, you passed out.
Zh: “有扇窗户被砸碎了。可能是录音机——歌停下来了——还有家具。真正的破坏。还有尖叫声。然后，我猜，你应该是晕过去了。”

En: Yes. She looks at you. "Never."
Zh: “是的。”她看着你。“永远不会。”

En: Was there anything else?
Zh: “还有其他事吗？”

En: Please tell me there wasn't anything *else*.
Zh: “请告诉我，没有*其他事*了。”

En: It prides me to hear this. Tell me there was more.
Zh: “听你这么说我感到很骄傲。告诉我，还有其他的对不对。”

En: There was. I think you screamed that you... didn't want to be 'this type of animal' anymore. I may have misheard, but it was sort of memorable.
Zh: “有的。我想你好像在喊……不想再做‘这种动物’了。我可能是听错了，不过确实挺难忘的。”

En: What happened then?
Zh: “后来发生什么了？”

En: I went out afterwards. Everything was quiet by then. Around four or five. She nods. "And that was it."
Zh: “后来我外出了。那时候一切都变得很安静。大概是4点或者5点的时候吧。”她点点头。“就这么多了。”

En: Don't back down now. Say what you said again -- *proudly*.
Zh: 别现在就打退堂鼓。再说一遍刚才的话--*骄傲*一点。

En: And why didn't you just tell me that?
Zh: “那你为什么没有直接告诉我呢？”

En: Whoah! Something wants to come out of you -- a bit of vomit? Thankfully you keep it down, because your body does not control you.
Zh: 哇哦！有什么东西想从你嘴里跑出来——有点想吐？谢天谢地，你忍住了，因为你的身体无法控制你。

En: No, she's not! Reassert yourself by being *flirtatious*.
Zh: 不，她没这么想！通过*调情*，再次重复自己的主张。

En: That feels like a jab, but honestly -- you deserve it. Plus, it would be *rude* to reprimand her.
Zh: 感觉像是挨了一拳，不过老实说——这是你应得的。再说了，谴责她实在是太*粗鲁*了。

En: Sounds like you were in some real *fundamental* pain there, muscle-man.
Zh: 听起来你感受到了真正*根本*的痛楚，肌肉男。

En: Did I have any visitors?
Zh: “有人来拜访过我吗？”

En: I couldn't say -- it's impossible to hear people speaking from over here. She nods toward Room #3.
Zh: “我说不好——从这里不可能听到别人说话的声音。”她朝着3号房点了点头。

En: Loud disco music?
Zh: “喧闹的迪斯科音乐？”

En: Tell me something else.
Zh: “跟我说点别的吧。”

En: Yeah, we go on, alright.
Zh: “对，生活总得继续。”

En: It mostly just gets hard, doesn't it?
Zh: “大多数情况下都会变得很难，不是吗？”

En: Maybe we should stop going on then? Doesn't the 'going on' cause the 'getting harder' part?
Zh: “那也许我们应该停下来？难道不是因为‘继续’导致‘越来越难’的吗？”

En: In that case, doesn't life get hard *because* we go on?
Zh: 如果那样的话，难道生活不就是*因为*我们的*继续*而变得越来越难吗？

En: I think you might have come to the same conclusion last night. At around two o'clock, the disco stopped, and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “我想你可能昨晚也得出了相同的结论。大概2点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停下来了，然后换了种节奏。”

En: I don't know about that... At around two o'clock, the disco stopped, and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “我不知道……大概2点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停下来了，然后换了种节奏。”

En: It would appear so. At around two o'clock, the disco stopped, and there was a change of pace.
Zh: “也许是吧。大概2点钟的时候，迪斯科音乐停下来了，然后换了种节奏。”

En: Oh yes, various artists. Ostentatious Orchestrations prime among them. She raises an eyebrow, waiting for the name to connect with you.
Zh: “没错，群星合辑。里面还有招摇乐团精选。”她抬起眉头，等着那个名字与你产生联系。

En: OO were *huge* where I come from. I was very young then, of course. Like seven. 'Life gets haaard!' she sings, "'But we go ooooooon!'"
Zh: “在我的家乡，招摇乐团简直是*超级巨星*。不过那时候我还很小。大概7岁的样子。‘生活不易！’”她唱了起来，“‘但我们还要继续！’”

En: Oh, *that*. Yeah, whooh... The less said about OO, the better.
Zh: 哦，*那个*。是啊……关于招摇乐团的讨论，越少越好。

En: Ah yes, *officer*... about that.
Zh: “是啊，*警官*……那个啊。”

En: Yes, sir?
Zh: “什么事，先生？”

En: How did you know I'm with the police?
Zh: “你怎么知道我是警官的？”

En: Unless you've been feeding us a set of well rehearsed lies, you've been here for three days. On *official police business* no less.
Zh: “除非你一直都在用精心编造的谎言欺骗我们。你已经在这里待了3天。你自己说，是来处理*警方事务*的。”

En: That's it.
Zh: “就这些。”

En: Good monologue! She smiles, flashing her pearl-white teeth.
Zh: “很不错的独角戏！”她笑着，露出珍珠一样白的牙齿。

En: You don't have to. No one is forcing you to be a cop.
Zh: 你不用这样的。没人逼你当警察。

En: *Try The Expression*? C'mon, why are you still doing this?
Zh: *尝试那个表情*？拜托，你为什么还要这么做？

En: The words have already left your mouth...
Zh: 话语已经从你嘴里蹦出来了……

En: Officer. The woman nods to you, still smoking her cigarette.
Zh: “警官。”女人朝你点点头，还在抽着烟。

En: I don't really remember being a cop, or anything else for that matter.
Zh: “我真的不记得自己是个警官，也不记得与此相关的任何事。”

En: I don't really remember being a cop, or anything else for that matter.
Zh: “我真的不记得自己是个警官，也不记得与此相关的任何事。”

En: Why don't I remember being a cop, or anything else?
Zh: “为什么我不记得自己是警官的事，还有别的事？”

En: I'm sorry. I was being glib. She pulls on her cigarette. "I'm sure you're a great cop."
Zh: “很抱歉。我有些油嘴滑舌。”她吸了一口烟。“我肯定你是个好警官。”

En: Extraordinary. She pulls on her cigarette and nods.
Zh: “很特别。”她吸了一口烟，点了点头。

En: And ever and ever... Out of the corner of your eye, you see the hair on the back of your hand standing on end. It feels as though low-grade lightning is coursing through you. You must be hypersensitive from the hangover.
Zh: 万万岁岁……从眼角的余光中，你看见自己手背上的汗毛已经立了起来。感觉就像是一道低强度的闪电穿过了你的身体。你一定是对宿醉过敏了。

En: Don't worry, I'm not going to vomit.
Zh: “别担心，我不会吐出来的。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: What kind of business?
Zh: “哪种事务？”

En: I can see that.
Zh: “我能看出来。”

En: Do you know why?
Zh: “你知道为什么吗？”

En: Good.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Is it? She raises an eyebrow. The cigarette sizzles.
Zh: “是吗？”她扬起眉头。手中的香烟嘶嘶作响。

En: Find someone else to laugh at.
Zh: “找个别的人嘲笑吧。”

En: In that case, I think I might have a *premonition* for you... The silence is foreboding as she extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “如果那样的话，我想我也许对你有一种*不祥的预感*……”她掐灭手中的香烟，沉默就像是一种预兆。

En: I'm sorry... She extinguishes her cigarette.
Zh: “我很抱歉……”她掐灭了手中的香烟。

En: Goodbye.
Zh: “再见。”

En: And no, it never stops.
Zh: “不会的，它永远不会停止。”

En: A silver jumpsuit falls off her like scale armour, sparkling. This is the sparkle of too many nights out on the city.
Zh: 她身着一件柔顺的银色连身裤，像鳞甲一样闪闪发光，仿佛城市夜晚的点点灯火。

En: She doesn't actually think it's all that extraordinary.
Zh: 她不是真心觉得很特别。

En: She remains unconvinced...
Zh: 她仍然没有被说服。

En: This is your morning cigarette. I deduced this from your lack of make-up. Also -- you *party*. A lot.
Zh: “这是你早晨的第一支烟。我是从你没化妆的样子推断出来的。而且——你喜欢*派对*。很喜欢。”

En: Not as much as you... She pulls on her cigarette. "I'm sorry, sir. I didn't mean to be glib. I'm sure you're very good at your job."
Zh: “没你那么喜欢……”她吸了一口烟。“很抱歉，长官。我不是故意这么油嘴滑舌的。我确定你很擅长自己的本职工作。”

En: Of course. She pulls on her cigarette.
Zh: “当然。”她吸了一口烟。

En: Isn't it the other way around? Heavy drinking decreases testosterone... and believe me, you would know.
Zh: 不应该正好相反吗？酗酒会导致睾丸素下降……相信我，你会知道的。

En: There's only one solution to this. You're a businessman.
Zh: 那只有一种解答了。你是一个商人。

En: All this time?
Zh: “一直以来都这样吗？”

En: You've been here for three days. On *official police business*, no less.
Zh: “你已经在这里待了3天。你自己说，是来处理*警方事务*的。”

En: She looks back at you, a light glinting off her eyes.
Zh: 她回望着你，眼里闪过一丝光亮。

En: Hello, officer.
Zh: “你好，长官。”

En: The door is closed.
Zh: 门是关着的。

En: Try the handle.
Zh: 试试把手。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: This door can only be opened with a key or from the inside.
Zh: 门只能用钥匙，或者是从里面打开。

En: As you hold the metal you sense the warmth left there by her hand.
Zh: 你握住金属手柄，感觉到她手心留下的温度。

En: There is no answer.
Zh: 没有回应。

En: You hear the shower being turned on, somewhere inside.
Zh: 你听见里面某个地方传来淋浴开启的声音。

En: A tremendous loneliness comes over you. Everybody in the world is doing something without you.
Zh: 一阵极大的孤独感向你袭来。世界上的每一个人都在做自己的事，但与你无关。

En: I'm so alone.
Zh: “我真的很孤独。”

En: Why are you doing this? Don't do this to me...
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？为什么要这样对我……”

En: Swallow the emotion.
Zh: 咽下这种情绪。

En: Knock again.
Zh: 再敲一次。

En: Still no answer.
Zh: 还是没有回应。

En: Still nothing.
Zh: 还是没反应。

En: Knock again, much harder.
Zh: 再敲一次，再重一点。

En: You should punch a fucking hole in it.
Zh: 你应该在里面砸个洞。

En: Fucking whore!
Zh: “该死的贱人！”

En: Punch the door.
Zh: 重重砸门。

En: Suppress the urge.
Zh: 抑制住冲动。

En: The murmur in your ears recedes slowly, your breathing normalizes. That's one lucky door.
Zh: 耳中的低语声慢慢褪去，你的呼吸变得正常起来。那扇门很幸运。

En: That felt good. Everyone is a whore. Now punch a hole in the door.
Zh: 那种感觉很好。所有人都是贱货。现在，在门上砸个洞吧。

En: Don't. Everyone is a whore? What does that even mean? Breathe in and walk away.
Zh: 别这样。所有人都是贱货？这句话到底是什么意思？深吸一口气，然后离开吧。

En: Breathe in.
Zh: 深吸一口气。

En: You hear the murmur of blood in your ears. Your back slowly relaxes when you breathe out. That's one lucky door.
Zh: 你听见血液在你耳边窃窃私语。当你呼出一口气的时候，你的后背慢慢放松下来。那扇门很幸运。

En: You slam your fist into the vinyl. It does not produce a hole. The door sits sturdily in the frame and your fist hurts. This was all a very good, normal thing to do.
Zh: 你用拳头狠狠地砸在乙烯基材料上，但是并没有制造出一个洞。门仍然牢牢地停留在门框里，你的拳头隐隐作痛。这是一件很好，也很正常的事。

En: The door is mute and indifferent. Your despair is a joke to it.
Zh: 门沉默而冷淡。对于它来说，你的绝望就是个笑话。

En: The door is indifferent to your loneliness. The world does not care.
Zh: 门对你的孤独表现地很冷淡。这个世界也毫不关心。

En: Beauty, don't abandon me in all this ugliness!
Zh: “美人，别把我留在这个丑陋的世界！”

En: The door does not care.
Zh: 门毫不关心。

En: The door probably thinks you're a has-been loser too. You feel your funny necktie becoming tighter and tighter around your neck...
Zh: 门可能也觉得你是个过气的失败者。你感觉脖子上那条可笑的领带越勒越紧……

En: The door is closed.
Zh: 门是关着的。

En: Not to mention *great* exercise!
Zh: 更不用说这是一种*超棒*的锻炼了！

En: The lieutenant gives you a quick glance.
Zh: 警督飞快地瞥了你一眼。

En: He doesn't like where this is going.
Zh: 他不喜欢这样。

En: Hey! What is your problem?
Zh: 喂！你有什么问题吗？

En: Stop that! The lieutenant sounds angry.
Zh: “停下来！”警督听起来很生气。

En: The door is closed.
Zh: 门是关着的。

En: Knock once more.
Zh: 再敲一次。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: There's still a dent in the vinyl -- where you punched it before.
Zh: 乙烯基材料上还留着一道凹痕——就在你刚才击中的位置。

En: Run your finger across the dent.
Zh: 用你的手指拂过凹痕。

En: This little depression is all you could inflict on the door.
Zh: 你只能给门留下这么一丁点的凹痕。

En: The words still echo in the hallway...
Zh: 话语仍然在走廊里回响……

En: Fucking whore.
Zh: 该死的贱人。

En: Just knock again.
Zh: 再敲一次。

En: There we go-- *fucking whore*. What a nice thing to say, Harry. Everything is A-Okay with you.
Zh: 好啊——*该死的贱人*。说得真好，哈里。你一切都很好。

En: The door feels cold.
Zh: 这扇门感觉冷冰冰的。

En: You're just as alone as the last time. Whatever kept you company in this world -- what kept you safe -- has left your side, never to return again.
Zh: 你和上次一样孤独。这个世界上陪伴着你的一切——这个世界上保护着你的一切——都抛弃了你，不再回来。

En: Why are you doing this? Don't do this to me, please! Remember? Who *was* that meant for?
Zh: “你为什么要这么做？别这样对我，拜托！”还记得吗？那个说的*是谁*？

En: Beauty has abandoned you in this ugliness, honcho. And then some! You cannot even remember the *name* of beauty; what beauty was...
Zh: 美丽把你一个人抛弃在这个丑陋的世界，兄弟。而且你甚至记不清美丽的*名字*；美丽是什么……

En: You swallow, like you swallowed the last time. Whatever bitter emotion was swimming to the surface then -- and now -- subsides. Submerges. Sinks back into you.
Zh: 你都咽了下去，就像上次一样。不管当时有怎样的苦涩情绪浮上表面——现在都已渐渐平息。将你淹没。深深沉入你的身体。

En: White Mourning. It was mourning all along. You should've seen it coming, it was right behind her eyes...
Zh: 白色哀悼。一直以来都是哀悼。你早就该预料到的，它就藏在她的双眸之后……

En: Knock...
Zh: 敲门……

En: Caustic Echo. It was darkness all along. But you were young and it was summer. You just had to take a dive...
Zh: 腐蚀性的回音。一直以来都是黑暗。但那时的你很年轻，而且是在一个夏季。你不得不认输……

En: Knock...
Zh: 敲门……

En: There is no answer -- it's nighttime, officer.
Zh: 没有回应——现在是半夜，警官。

En: A young woman like this is probably out partying the night away. Drink and drugs, sweet drugs are involved. Loneliness begone...
Zh: 像这样的年轻女人可能会整晚在外面狂欢。那里有酒精，还有甜美的毒品。孤独也会远去……

En: In the Jamrock Quarter, The Paliseum for music, company and men... or the Door To The River, for death and synthetic opiates. Or, way in the East in Couron, in a starlit *boîte de nuits* like *La Cherte*... far from here and you, shivering dog.
Zh: 在加姆洛克住宅区，音乐、公司和人类的帕里瑟姆……或者是河流的门户，为了死亡和人造麻醉剂。又或者，远在科戎东部，像*榭赫蒂*一样星光闪耀的*夜总会*……远离这里，远离你，这条颤抖的老狗。

En: We should come back in the morning.
Zh: “我们应该早上再回来。”

En: Who is it? A woman's voice answers -- muffled by the door.
Zh: “是谁？”一个女人的声音响起——隔着门有些听不清。

En: Tired. Controlled.
Zh: 疲倦。克制。

En: This is the police. Can we come in?
Zh: “我们是警察。可以进来吗？”

En: Somewhere not far from here the lieutenant is preparing her file. "Klaasje Amandou," he writes. You realize the detective should be here for what comes next. It could prove... *dangerous* to go in alone.
Zh: 就在离这里不远的某个地方，警督正在准备她的文件。“卡拉洁·阿曼多，”他书写着。你意识到接下来要发生的事，警探也应该待在这里。孤身一个人进去……可能很*危险*。

En: Come on up -- the door is open! She shouts: "I'm drying my hair..."
Zh: “进来吧——门是开的！”她大喊到：“我在吹头发……”

En: Sounds like it's coming from upstairs somewhere -- you could snoop around before going up...
Zh: 听起来像是来自楼上的某个地方——在上去之前，你可以巡视一下……

En: Seems she left in a hurry.
Zh: “看来她离开的很匆忙。”

En: Seems she left in a hurry.
Zh: 看来她离开的很匆忙。

En: You look at her rough, jagged handwriting on the note. She was in a rush, but not panicked. Deliberate. Focused.
Zh: 你看着便条上参差不齐又潦草的字迹。她很匆忙，但是并不恐慌。深思熟虑。十分专注。

En: But not panicked. Deliberate. Focused.
Zh: 但是并不恐慌。深思熟虑。十分专注。

En: Just don't walk into another radio trap, okay...
Zh: 只要千万别走进另一个无线电陷阱，好吗……

En: Relax. Not everyone is out to trap you.
Zh: 放松。不是所有人都在给你设陷阱的。

En: A gift, huh... ? The kid turns to the staircase. "That's fucking suspicious to Cuno."
Zh: “一个礼物，嗯……？”男孩转向楼梯。“在坤诺看来，这他妈也太可疑了。”

En:
I'm sorry I fucked everyone over.

PS. I didn't kill him.

PPS. Gift upstairs.
Zh:
“很抱歉，我骗了所有人。

另.我没杀他。

又另.楼上有礼物。”

En:
See you at The Return.

PS. I didn't kill him.

PPS. Gift upstairs.
Zh:
“归来的时候再见。

另.我没杀他。

又另.楼上有礼物。”

En: Next to stacks of bills, you see a note -- a few lines jotted down in large, uneven handwriting just as the writer was about to rush out the door.
Zh: 在成堆的账单旁边，你看见一份笔记——上面留下了几行字，字迹又大又潦草，好像笔者马上要冲出门去。

En: A gift... The lieutenant turns to the staircase suspiciously, looking for any signs of another presence in the shadows above.
Zh: “一份礼物……”警督怀疑地转向楼梯，在上方的阴影中寻找其他存在的痕迹。

En: What could this *gift* be?
Zh: “这个*礼物*会是什么？”

En: What could this *gift* be?
Zh: 这个*礼物*会是什么？

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En:
See you at The Return.

PS. I didn't kill him.

PPS. Gift upstairs.
Zh:
“很抱歉，我骗了所有人。

另.我没杀他。

又另.楼上有礼物。”

En:
I'm sorry I fucked everyone over.

PS. I didn't kill him.

PPS. Gift upstairs.
Zh: “很抱歉，我骗了所有人。回见。另.我没杀他。又另.楼上有礼物。”

En: I am not drawing my gun... yet. But I don't like 'gifts'.
Zh: “我不会拔枪的……暂时不会。但我不喜欢*礼物*。”

En: He says he's not, but his hand moves instinctively toward his holster as he studies the note.
Zh: 他说他不会，但看着字条的时候，却本能地把手伸向了枪套的方向。

En: It's hardly surprising.
Zh: “这没什么好奇怪的。”

En: The note does not reply. The jagged, rough handwriting looks like she was in a hurry.
Zh: 便条没有回复。那些参差不齐又潦草的字迹看起来像是她在赶时间。

En: No response, not even an echo in the stuffy room. You notice a silk shirt on the stairs.
Zh: 这个不透气的房间里没有响应，甚至没有一点回音。你注意到楼梯上有一件丝绸衬衫。

En: Yeah-yeah. He looks around. "Fugitive on the run shit. Cuno's seen this shit before."
Zh: “是啊是啊。”他环顾四周。“逍遥法外的逃犯什么的。坤诺之前见过这种破事。”

En: A trap, probably. He nods confidently. "But don't sweat it. You got the Cuno spotting you. Let's go in."
Zh: “一个陷阱，有可能。”他自信地点点头。“但是别担心，猪头。还有坤诺赏识你。我们进去吧。”

En: A bright orange bottle with PREPTIDE stamped on it. In sunny, happy letters! Jackpot, baby! That's the stuff you're looking for! Your palms begin to sweat just holding that little plastic container.
Zh: 一个亮橙色的瓶子，上面印着帕立太特的标志。多么阳光而愉悦的字母啊！你中头奖了，宝贝！这就是你在找的东西！仅仅是握住这个小塑料容器，就已经让你的手心开始出汗了。

En: The cabinet is cleaned out, emptied.
Zh: 橱柜已经清理过了，是空的。

En: Probably Garte's work. After she was arrested.
Zh: 也许是加尔特做的。在她被逮捕之后。

En: This toothbrush belongs to someone who doesn't give a damn about her teeth.
Zh: 这只牙刷属于某个根本不在乎自己牙齿的人。

En: Who cares, man? Who cares what the stiff thinks? Here comes the afterburner! Just put it in your sweaty little hand now...
Zh: 谁在乎啊呢，伙计？谁在乎那个呆瓜怎么想的？助力器来了！现在，只要把它握在你那汗津津的小手里……

En: Be honest. He's not grilling you, he just wants to know. Ask if he's ever wanted to take it too.
Zh: 说实在的。他没有责问你，他只是想知道。问问他有没有想过也来一点。

En: This medicine cabinet is full of wares -- sheets of pills haphazardly stacked one on top of the other. There's also a toothbrush somewhere in there.
Zh: 药柜里塞满了东西——成堆的药片杂乱地堆叠在一起。里面甚至还有一把牙刷。

En: Look at the toothbrush.
Zh: 看看牙刷。

En: There's quite the collection in here.
Zh: “这里的藏品还真是够多啊。”

En: [Close the cabinet.]
Zh: [关上柜子。]

En: Look at the medications.
Zh: 看着药物。

En: It's been used. Quite a lot.
Zh: 用过了，而且经常用。

En: But not for scrubbing blood off tiles -- or anything else interesting, it seems.
Zh: 这不是用来擦洗瓷砖上的血迹的——好像也没什么其他有意思的用途。

En: It's clean of chemical odours too. Just some dried toothpaste on the handle.
Zh: 它没有什么化学气味。只是手柄上还残留着一些干涸的牙膏印。

En: Pharmaceuticals line the shelves. Sheet upon sheet and pill bottle next to pill bottle: acetylic acid, APAP, eye drops, blood thinners...
Zh: 架子上全是药物。成堆成堆的药瓶一个接着一个：乙酰基酸，扑热息痛，眼药水，血液稀释剂……

En: Anything of note? he asks in a lowered voice.
Zh: “有什么值得注意的吗？”他低声问到。

En: Search the bottles.
Zh: 搜查瓶子。

En: Pill bottles rattle like bones as you search the cabinet: paracetamol, histaperidol, something in a foreign language you can't read... Behind them: an unusually shaped nasal spray. Its label reads 'NACRA'.
Zh: 你在药柜中翻找着，药瓶像骨骼一样发出嘎吱嘎吱的响声：醋氨酚，氟哌啶醇，还有个写着外文看不懂的东西……在那后面：是一个形状怪异的喷鼻剂。标签上写着“纳呵拉”。

En: NACRA? This is used to treat opioid overdoses. Always handy to have around.
Zh: 纳呵拉？这是用来治疗阿片类中毒的。带在身边总是挺方便的。

En: Among some foreign -- probably Messinian, or Gottwaldian -- marked red blister packs you find... what *do* you find? This is going to take a little know-how.
Zh: 你找到些有外文标记的——可能是墨西拿，或者是戈特瓦汀——红色吸塑包……你发现*什么*了？这可能需要一点专业技巧。

En: (Whisper.) "What's NACRA?"
Zh: （窃窃私语。）“纳呵拉是什么？”

En: (Whisper.) "NACRA, opioid antagonist."
Zh: （窃窃私语。）“纳呵拉，阿片类拮抗剂。”

En: Search the pill sheets.
Zh: 寻找药品说明书。

En: An opioid receptor antagonist. It's used for diamorphine overdoses.
Zh: “一种阿片受体拮抗剂。用来治疗海洛因中毒的。”

En: Interesting. That's used for diamorphine overdoses... The lieutenant nods -- then looks at the door.
Zh: “有意思。这是用来治疗海洛因中毒的……”警督点点头——然后看向大门。

En: And your chest swells with pride -- look! It's made right here in Revachol, by Saint-Batiste, a proud Revacholian pharmaceutical company with great *traditions*!
Zh: 你的胸中满是骄傲——看啦！这是瑞瓦肖本地产的，圣巴蒂斯特——一间骄傲的瑞瓦肖制药公司，拥有相当伟大的*传统*！

En: What was so exciting about the orange bottle?
Zh: 这个橙色瓶子有什么好兴奋的？

En: What's so exciting about this orange bottle?
Zh: 这个橙色瓶子有什么好兴奋的？

En: Lieutenant, I also see a brand called 'Preptide.'
Zh: “警督，我还看见一个商标，叫‘帕立太特’。”

En: It's speed, man! Just what you were looking for. They call it *dextroamphetamine* and talk about psychological disorders, but what we're seeing here is some scientifically advanced trucker speed!
Zh: 是思必得，伙计！这就是你在找的东西。大家都叫它*右旋安非他命*，而且还谈到了心理障碍，但是我们在这里看到的，是技术更先进的盐酸麻黄素。

En: Preptide -- a euphemism for pharmaceutical amphetamine. Prescription speed.
Zh: “帕立太特——是安非他命的一种委婉说法。处方思必得。”

En: The fuck are you waiting for, let's get super fucking PREPPIE!!!
Zh: 你他妈还在等什么啊，让我们像大学预科生一样赶紧嗨起来啊！！！

En: Take it -- in secret.
Zh: 拿走它——偷偷的。

En: Told Kim about it.
Zh: 告诉金。

En: Sweaty hands.
Zh: 手心全是汗。

En: Need for speed.
Zh: 需求高速。

En: Just take it in plain sight.
Zh: 光明正大的拿走它。

En: [Close the cabinet.]
Zh: [关上柜子。]

En: Silently the bottle slides into your palm, then -- as you turn -- into your pocket.
Zh: 瓶子静悄悄地滑入你的掌心，然后——你转过身——它掉进了你的口袋里。

En: You dropped it. Smack dab in the middle of the floor! There it is -- orange as the midsummer evening's sun, or the lieutenant's bomber jacket.
Zh: 你弄掉了。啪的一声掉在了地板中央！它就在那里——像仲夏黄昏的太阳，或者警督的飞行员夹克一样，橙黄锃亮。

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 自己保密。

En: The lieutenant glances at the rows of pills -- then at the door.
Zh: 警督瞥了一眼成排的药片——然后看着大门的方向。

En: Feels like gold in your hands. Light and precious.
Zh: 在你手中像黄金一样。轻巧而珍贵。

En: The lieutenant looks toward the door as if he didn't see you pilfer that sweet Preptide.
Zh: 警督看着大门的方向，好像没发现你偷走了那份甜美的帕立太特。

En: He did, though. He totally did and now he thinks less of you. Should have kept it *sub rosa*.
Zh: 其实他看到了。绝对看到了，现在他对你的评价又降低了。应该*偷偷*藏起来的。

En: Wow. That's unbelievably orange. *Anyone* can see that. It's practically *effervescent*!
Zh: 哇哦。真是难以置信的橙黄色。*所有人*都能看见。它几乎*沸腾*了一般！

En: Pick it up.
Zh: 把它捡起来。

En: Pick it up -- but secretly.
Zh: 捡起来——偷偷摸摸的。

En: The lieutenant *tries* to keep his eyes on the door, but the Preptide bottle sounds like a rattlesnake -- it's basically a percussion instrument that you're stuffing into your pocket.
Zh: 警督*试着*把视线停留在大门上，但是帕立太特的瓶子发出了响尾蛇一般的声响——你基本上是把一个打击乐器塞进自己的口袋了。

En: This medicine cabinet is stocked with drugs, plus an old toothbrush, and...
Zh: 药柜里塞满药品，加上一只旧牙刷，还有……

En: ...nothing of note. The further you look the more arcane it gets. You don't know what the rest of this stuff does.
Zh: ……没什么值得注意的东西。你看得越多，它就变得越神秘。你不知道这些东西有什么用。

En: Is there something more interesting here?
Zh: 还有什么更有意思的东西吗？

En: Chemical hangover.
Zh: 化学宿醉。

En: A hound of narcomania.
Zh: 麻醉剂上瘾的困扰。

En: Magnetic Dice Set.
Zh: 磁性骰子套组。

En: Dresscode: Modus Mullen.
Zh: 着装要求：模式马伦。

En: The medicine cabinet is empty. Not even a toothbrush.
Zh: 药柜是空的。甚至连牙刷都没有。

En: The medicine cabinet has been cleaned out. Nothing remains, not one pill or bottle.
Zh: 药柜已经被清理一空。里面什么也没剩下，一片药，一个瓶子都没有。

En: Pity.
Zh: 可惜。

En: I was kinda hoping the *gift* would be in here.
Zh: “我还有点希望*礼物*就在里面呢。”

En: She's really cleaned this out.
Zh: “她真的把一切都清干净了。”

En: [Close the cabinet.]
Zh: [关上柜子。]

En: Mhm, the lieutenant nods. "She certainly had her priorities straight when she was packing."
Zh: “嗯，”警督点点头。“在收拾行李的时候，她肯定有自己的优先事项。”

En: Of course you were. He nods. "How does that work anyway?"
Zh: “你当然是了。”他点点头。“那到底是怎么运作的？”

En: How does what work?
Zh: “什么怎么运作？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: The lieutenant does not pursue the subject further, looking out the bathroom window instead.
Zh: 警督没有继续追究这个话题，而是看向浴室的窗外。

En: Amphetamine -- does it make you a better detective?
Zh: “安非他命——它会让你变成一个更优秀的警探吗？”

En: What are you talking about? I'm not a narcomaniac, don't be *ludicrous*!
Zh: “你说什么呢？我不是个瘾君子，别*开玩笑*了！”

En: What are you talking about? I don't take amphetamine, don't be *ludicrous*!
Zh: “你说什么呢？我没有吃安非他命，别*开玩笑*了！”

En: It keeps me going -- in the short run.
Zh: “它能让我继续前进——短期内是这样的。”

En: I always took you for more of a drunk than a chemical abuser, lieutenant-yefreitor, he says as though its self-evident. "Should we go?"
Zh: “我一直觉得你更像是个醉汉，而不是滥用药物的人，荣誉警督，”他说的好像不言而喻一样。“我们可以走了吧？”

En: I always took you for more of a drunk than a chemical abuser, he says as though it's self-evident. "Anyway."
Zh: “我一直觉得你更像是个醉汉，而不是滥用药物的人，”他说的好像不言而喻一样。“随便你吧。”

En: Narcomania is a disease, lieutenant.
Zh: “安非他命是一种疾病，警督。”

En: It may be -- but I asked if it makes you better at your work?
Zh: “有可能——但是我想问他让你的工作表现更出色了吗？”

En: There is little condescension in his phrasing, more measured curiosity.
Zh: 他的措辞里几乎没有傲慢的感觉，更多是审慎的好奇心。

En: I was just curious. Anyway let's go.
Zh: “我只是好奇。不管怎么说，我们走吧。”

En: Honesty, it makes me the detective I am. Have you thought of taking it too?
Zh: “说实在的，它让我成为了现在的警探。你有没有想过也来一点？”

En: It has no effect on my performance, I just take it to feel good.
Zh: “它对我的表现没有任何影响，只是让我感觉比较好。”

En: Better? No. It makes me worse.
Zh: “更好？没有。让我感觉更糟糕了。”

En: Hmh. He lets out a little pensive hum... "That's what I thought. Let's go?"
Zh: “嗯。”他发出一声略带忧郁的哼声……“我也是这么想的。我们走吧？”

En: Maybe I should? He lets out a little pensive hum, rubbing his shoulder...
Zh: “也许我应该吧？”他发出一声略带忧郁的哼声，稍微揉了揉肩膀……

En: It's not insincere. He's actually giving it thought.
Zh: 一点都不诚恳。他其实就是在考虑。

En: The beating he took must be more serious than he lets on.
Zh: 他挨的那一下子肯定比表现出来的更严重。

En: Hmh. He lets out a little pensive hum. "Thought so. Let's go?"
Zh: “嗯。”他发出一声略带忧郁的哼声。“我想也是。我们走吧？”

En: So... the good lieutenant was looking for an edge, was he?
Zh: 所以……我们这位好警督也在边缘试探，不是吗？

En: Hmh. He lets out a little pensive hum. "Thought so. Let's go?"
Zh: “嗯。”他发出一声略带忧郁的哼声。“我想也是。我们走吧？”

En: Doesn't the... pupils and the gurning jaw, the sweating... doesn't it become tiring after a while?
Zh: “难道不是……瞳孔和下巴歪曲，还有出汗……不久就会变得疲惫吗？”

En: Nah, man, the jaw grinding and the sweating -- that's the best part. Shows the medicine is working.
Zh: “不，伙计，下巴歪曲和出汗——这才是最棒的部分。代表药物正在起作用。”

En: I understand it's unbecoming but if I don't perform this job well I am nothing. It's the price I pay.
Zh: “我知道这不合适，但是如果我的工作表现不好的话，那我就一无所有了。这是我必须付出的代价。”

En: I'm too high on pyrholidon to notice. Before you ask -- pyrholidon is used for radiation sickness.
Zh: “我嗑吡嚯浣酮上头了。在你开口问之前——吡嚯浣酮是用来治疗辐射病的。”

En: Yes, but... did you know you don't *have* radiation sickness?
Zh: “是的，但是……你知道你*没有*辐射病吗？”

En: It gives me fiery visions. A window into the heart of things.
Zh: “它能让我看到炽烈的幻象。它是一扇通往万物之心的窗户。”

En: Yes, that does sound dangerous now that I think about it.
Zh: “是的，现在想来，好像是挺危险的。”

En: Right. I'll give it some thought. He seems satisfied with your answer. "Should we go?"
Zh: “好吧。我会考虑一下的。”他好像对你的答案很满意。“我们可以走了吗？”

En: Right. Thank you for this information. He seems satisfied. "Should we go?"
Zh: “好吧。感谢你提供的信息。”他好像很满意。“我们可以走了吗？”

En: Looking like a beast in heat is a serious downside, yes.
Zh: “看来发情的野兽是一个严重的缺点，没错。”

En: I thought so. He seems satisfied with the answer. "Should we go?"
Zh: “我也这么觉得。”他好像对你的答案很满意。“我们可以走了吗？”

En: Right. He seems satisfied with your answer. "I can see how that can be useful."
Zh: “好吧。”他好像对你的答案很满意。“我能看出来它为什么有用了。”

En: There's always a price... He looks out the window.
Zh: “一切都有代价的……”他看向窗外。

En: You're just talking to your reflection in the cabinet doors now... Things are not good with you.
Zh: 你现在只是在跟橱柜门上自己的倒影说话……情况好像不太好。

En: Feels so sleek and beautiful -- you got it! Here comes the afterburner! Time to become a super-detective again, Harry! Just put it in your sweaty little hand now...
Zh: 感觉如此圆滑和美丽——你拿到了！助力器来了！是时候再次变回那个超级警探了，哈里！现在，只要把它握在你那汗津津的小手里……

En: Who cares, man? Who cares what the stiff thinks? You got it. Here comes the afterburner! Just put it in your sweaty little hand now...
Zh: 谁在乎，老兄？谁在乎那个呆瓜怎么想的？你可以的。助力器来了！是时候再次变回那个超级警探了，哈里！现在，只要把它握在你那汗津津的小手里……

En: Nah, pig... the kid says looking around like a young wolf. "That'd been too easy. Let's keep looking."
Zh: “不，猪头……”男孩一边说着，一边像幼狼一样环顾四周。“那样就太简单了。我们还是继续找吧。”

En: Smart chick. The kid nods. "Cuno never leaves shit behind either. Cuno's a pro."
Zh: “很聪明的小妞。”男孩点点头。“坤诺从来不会留下任何东西。坤诺是专业的。”

En: Filthy narco! And look -- it's made right here in Revachol, by Saint-Batiste. A disgraceful pharmaceutical company attempting to lure people into the gutter. You should destroy it!
Zh: 肮脏的毒贩！看啦——它是瑞瓦肖本地的圣巴蒂斯特制造的。真是一家可耻的制药公司，想要引诱别人堕落。你应该毁掉它！

En: Yes, take it... and then dispose of it! This den of narcomania cannot be allowed stand!
Zh: 是的，拿走它……然后除掉它！不能让这种制毒窝点存活下来！

En: You could say he's on the *lookout*.
Zh: 你可以说他在*放哨*。

En: Search for the Preptide again.
Zh: 再次搜寻帕立太特。

En: There it is, the orange sun wearing blister pack. You feel almost *nostalgic* for it.
Zh: 它就在这里，穿着吸塑装的橙色太阳。你几乎产生了某种*怀旧*的感觉。

En: You dropped it. Smack dab in the middle of the floor! There it is -- orange as the midsummer evening's sun.
La Parbat, 26° centigrade, arid.
Zh: “拉尔巴特，26摄氏度，干旱。”

En: Vredefort, 15° centigrade, dull.
Zh: “伍德福特，15摄氏度，阴天。”

En: Deora, 19° centigrade, clear.
Zh: “德奥拉，19摄氏度，晴天。”

En: Thylakos-by-Pisantique, 28° centigrade, limited rainfall.
Zh: “泰拉科斯-皮桑缇克，28摄氏度，局部有雨。”

En: Loos, 11° centigrade, rainfall.
Zh: “洛斯，11摄氏度，有雨。”

En: Revachol (Zone of Control), 0° centigrade, black ice warning.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖（控制区），0度，黑冰预警。”

En: Shestaprel, -9° centigrade, overcast.
Zh: “谢斯普尔,零下9摄氏度，多云。”

En: Igaunas, -4° centigrade, bad road conditions.
Zh: “伊格纳斯,零下4摄氏度，路况不佳。”

En: Vaasa, -12° centigrade, hail.
Zh: “瓦萨城，零下4摄氏度，冰雹。”

En: Seol Cité, not available.
Zh: “西奥城，信息不详。”

En: Yelinka, -44° centigrade, snowstorm.
Zh: “叶琳卡，零下44摄氏度，暴风雪。”

En: Lohdu, Uuhu Reservation, -22° centigrade, snowdrifts.
Zh: “洛杜乌胡保留地，零下44摄氏度，小雪。”

En: Stepankedra, 9° centigrade, anafront.
Zh: “西潘肯德拉，9摄氏度，上滑锋”

En: Ilal-Sahrava, 52° centigrade, sun breaks and starbursts.
Zh: “伊纳拉-撒哈瓦，52摄氏度，晴”

En:
Mirova, 20° centigrade, partial cloud cover.	“米诺瓦，20摄氏度，局部多云”
0x0100003700000125	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F2 / TAPE PLAYER	Half Light	Fuck is that going to achieve? Being a smart-ass... making your nostrils flare.	这他妈能实现吗？当个自以为是的家伙……让你的鼻孔都张开了。
0x0100006B00001F6D	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F2 / TAPE PLAYER	Stereo 8 Player	The compact tape player is still and silent. Seems it has completely broken down now.	小型录音机一动不动，沉默不语。好像已经完全坏掉了。
0x0100006B00001F74	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F2 / TAPE PLAYER	Interfacing	There is no fixing this one.	这个是修不好了。
0x0100006B00001F81	Dialogue Text	WHIRLING F2 / TAPE PLAYER	Kim Kitsuragi	This would have been *very* helpful with the 'Megamix'." He stares at the tape player. "But it isn't anymore."
Zh: “如果要播‘精选集’的话，这个也许会*很有*帮助。”他盯着录音机。“不过现在不行了。”

En: Yeah... any ideas?
Zh: “是啊……有什么想法吗？”

En: It's not *my* fault.
Zh: “这不是*我的*错。”

En: Fuck off, Kim.
Zh: “去你的吧，金。”

En: My Kineema only comes with a radio, he ponders. "We should try to find a new tape player. There's a pawnshop near the water lock, south of here. Maybe they have a spare."
Zh: “我的锐影只有一台无线电，”他沉思着。“我们应该找台新录音机。南边的水闸附近有一间当铺。也许那里还有多余的。”

En: My Kineema only comes with a radio, he ponders. "Let's try to find a new tape player. Perhaps we should talk to Roy at the pawnshop -- he has stuff."
Zh: “我的锐影只有一台无线电，”他沉思着。“我们应该找台新录音机。也许我们应该找当铺的罗伊聊聊——他那里有不少东西。”

En: What should I do now, Stereo 8 Player?
Zh: 我现在该怎么做，8道立体声录音机？

En: The broken tape player doesn't have the ability to respond. But Kim would.
Zh: 坏掉的录音机没有回应的能力。但是金可以。

En: Great. What now?
Zh: 很好。现在怎么办？

En: Well shit.
Zh: 真该死。

En: Best find a new player. A boombox, funky style? Maybe the guy at the pawnshop can help out?
Zh: 最好找一台新录音机。来个时髦点的手提音箱？也许当铺的那个家伙可以帮忙？

En: Of course, he notes. "We should try to find a new tape player. Perhaps we can talk to Roy at the pawnshop south of here -- he had stuff."
Zh: “当然，”他说道。“我们应该找台新录音机。也许可以找南边当铺的罗伊聊聊——他那里有不少东西。”

En: Of course, he notes. "Anyway, we should try to find a new tape player. There's a pawnshop near the water lock, south of here. Maybe they have a spare."
Zh: “当然，”他说道。“不管怎么说，我们应该找台新录音机。南边的水闸附近有一间当铺。也许那里还有多余的。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't even flinch. "Anyway, let's try to find a new tape player. There's a pawnshop near the water lock, maybe they have a spare."
Zh: 警督毫无畏惧。“不管怎么说，我们应该找台新录音机。南边的水闸附近有一间当铺，也许那里还有多余的。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't even flinch. "Anyway, let's try to find a new tape player. Perhaps we should talk to Roy at the pawnshop."
Zh: 警督毫无畏惧。“不管怎么说，我们应该找台新录音机。也许我们应该找当铺的罗伊聊聊。”

En: The machine was made in Revachol by a company called Lemercier -- their logo depicts the triple-towered Delta skyline. It was supposedly built to last.
Zh: 这台机器是瑞瓦肖生产的，公司叫莱默西埃——标志上描绘着德尔塔的三重天际线。可能是想表达基业长青。

En: I guess the only thing to do is to find a new player. Might be one waiting for you out there somewhere.
Zh: 我猜唯一能做的就是找到一台新录音机。也许它就在外面的某个地方等着你。

En: (His voice echoes from downstairs.) "What the hell is going on up there?"
Zh: （他的声音在楼下回响。）“上面他妈的出什么事了？”

En: The lieutenant looks at you with a mixture of pity and weariness. "Come on, let's go down."
Zh: 警督看着你，脸上混杂着同情和疲倦的表情。“走吧，我们下去。”

En: Just a moment. You had some questions earlier, I believe, and, besides, we should discuss our progress on the investigation. Let's go out to the balcony.
Zh: “等等。我记得你之前说有些问题，而且我们还要聊聊调查的进展。去阳台外面吧。”

En: Just a moment. We should talk about our progress on the investigation. Let's go out to the balcony.
Zh: “等等。我们还要聊聊调查的进展。去阳台外面吧。”

En: The door to your room locks every night at 21.00 if you haven't paid for the room. You can pay downstairs when speaking to Garte, the cafeteria manager.
Zh: 如果不付房费，房间会在每晚21点上锁。去楼下找餐厅经理加尔特说话的时候可以支付房费。

En: You could try climbing into that trash bin in the yard, hobocop style...
Zh: 你可以试着爬进院子里的那个垃圾桶里，这是流浪警探的风格……

En: You could try climbing into that trash bin in the yard, hobocop style... after you've gotten open the lid.
Zh: 你可以试着爬进院子里的那个垃圾箱里，这是流浪警探的风格……特别是在你把盖子之后。

En: Hey, remember that trash bin idea...? Time to get climbing!
Zh: 嗯，还记得垃圾箱的点子吗……？是时候爬进去了！

En: Something about it has changed.
Zh: 有些东西已经变了。

En: Try to unlock the door.
Zh: 试着打开门。

En: Your key no longer turns in the lock.
Zh: 你的钥匙拧不开锁了。

En: It must be an electronic lock. You will have to get Garte, the cafeteria manager, to open it first -- after you've paid for the night.
Zh: “这肯定是电子锁。你得去找加尔特，那个餐厅经理，先把它打开——当然是在你付清今晚的房费之后。”

En: You feel uncertain, like a child who's lost his mother in the crowd.
Zh: 你感到疑惑，就像在人群中与母亲失散的小孩。

En: Why would I want to stay in such a place?
Zh: 我为什么想待在这种地方？

En: Still sounds better than sleeping outside.
Zh: 听起来还是比睡在外面好多了。

En: Because it's your last lifeline to being a police officer instead of a hobo.
Zh: 因为这是最后一条救生索——这样你还能当个警察，而不是流浪汉。

En: Yes, and it's not just a matter of comfort, but also of principle. This may be your last lifeline to being a police officer instead of a hobo.
Zh: 是的，而且这不仅仅与舒适有关，还跟原则有关。这很可能是你最后一条救生索——这样你还能当个警察，而不是流浪汉。

En: Just punch the damned door. No *locks* are going to keep you out.
Zh: 用拳头拼命砸门。没什么*锁*可以把你关在外面。

En: Nah, I'll get by somehow. Didn't like that room very much anyway.
Zh: “算了，我总会想到办法的。反正我也不是很喜欢那个房间。”

En: Actually, I don't need to pay -- I will brave the streets. Go undercover as a hobocop.
Zh: “其实呢，我不需要给钱——我会勇敢地走上街头。扮成流浪警察去卧底。”

En: Right, I should go pay my debts before I can go to sleep.
Zh: “好吧，我得先还清自己的债务，然后才能睡觉。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Our investigation does not at present require for anyone to go undercover. Let's drop this discussion and go settle your debt with the manager.
Zh: “我们目前的调查不需要任何人去卧底。不用多说，直接找经理解决你的债务问题吧。”

En: I would advise against that. It's already late, and you won't find cheaper accommodation in the vicinity.
Zh: “我会建议不要这么做。现在已经不早了，在附近你也找不到更便宜的住宿地点。”

En: He nods. "Yes, we both need to get proper rest tonight."
Zh: 他点点头。“是啊，今晚我们都需要好好休息。”

En: He's asserting his leadership talents because he is concerned about you.
Zh: 他在维护自己的领导才能，因为他关心你。

En: Observe the damage to your knuckles. Is this really worth it?
Zh: 检查指关节受的伤。真的值得吗？

En: The lieutenant looks on impassively. "Even if you could break down the door, all you would get is another bill."
Zh: 警督冷漠地看着你。“就算你能把门砸开，也只会得到另一张账单而已。”

En: *Even if you could*? Don't let him talk you down, son!
Zh: *就算你能*？别让他这么居高临下的跟你说话，小子！

En: Try one more time. This time with both fists, one after the other.
Zh: 再试一次。这次用上两个拳头，一个接一个。

En: (His voice echoes from downstairs.) "What's going on up there?"
Zh: （他的声音在楼下回响。）“上面出什么事了？”

En: The lieutenant purses his lips. "Admirable form. Now come on, let's go down."
Zh: 警督咬紧嘴唇。“真是令人钦佩的方式。走吧，我们下去。”

En: (His voice echoes from downstairs.) "What the hell is going on up there?"
Zh: （他的声音在楼下回响。）“上面他妈的出什么事了？”

En: The lieutenant looks at you with a mixture of pity and weariness. "Come on, let's go down."
Zh: 警督看着你，脸上混杂着同情和疲倦的表情。“走吧，我们下去。”

En: Aaaaa! It huuurts!
Zh: “啊啊啊啊！好疼！”

En: Keep it in. Cradle your arm.
Zh: 忍住。拖住你的手臂。

En: Try to stay cool.
Zh: 试着保持冷静。

En: Nice try. The tears come streaming down your face as you crumble to the ground.
Zh: 不错的尝试。你崩溃地倒向地面，泪水从脸颊不断滑落。

En: Blubber. And keep blubbering.
Zh: 哭闹，继续哭闹。

En: The tears bring some relief, but not much.
Zh: 泪水带来些许安慰，但并不太多。

En: Your angry voice carries through the Whirling, but you still can't stop the tears from welling up in your eyes...
Zh: 你愤怒的声音传遍了整个飞旋旅社，但仍然无法阻止从眼里涌出的泪水……

En: This is the door to the room you *redecorated*.
Zh: 这是你*重新装饰*过的房间大门。

En: This is the door to your room.
Zh: 这是你的房间大门。

En: Punch the door.
Zh: 用力砸门。

En: The door rings from the impact, but does not budge.
Zh: 门因为冲击而发出回响，但却依然纹丝不动。

En: Your fist bounces off the door and comes back to you on a wave of agony that surges up your arm.
Zh: 你的拳头从门上弹了回来，一阵痛楚涌上你的整条手臂。

En: It hurts, but keep your cool. You've got this.
Zh: 真的很疼，但是一定要保持冷静。你能做到的。

En: (His voice echoes from downstairs.) "What's going on up there?"
Zh: （他的声音在楼下回响。）“上面出什么事了？”

En: Pow-pow! That was impressive.
Zh: 砰砰！还真是令人印象深刻啊。

En: This door will fall prostrate before The Law! (Punch the door again.)
Zh: “这扇门一定会拜倒在法律面前！”（再次砸门。）

En: The door shudders, but appears undamaged.
Zh: 门颤抖着，但好像没受到什么伤害。

En: The door appears undamaged, and even more impassive than the lieutenant.
Zh: 门似乎完好无损，看起来甚至比警督还要冷漠。

En: You've got this. Men like you don't cry unless it serves their purposes.
Zh: 你可以的。像你这样的男人是不会轻易流泪的，除非有所目的。

En: Confront the door with a stoical expression. You are still The Law.
Zh: 摆出坚忍的表情与门对垒。你依然代表着法律。

En: He nods toward the staircase. "Come on, let's go down."
Zh: 他朝着楼梯点点头。“来吧，我们下去。”

En: Good night, lieutenant.
Zh: “晚安，警督。”

En: Alright, let's go.
Zh: “好吧，我们走。”
